title.raw: 卷十下 皇后紀 Volume 10b: Annals of Empresses 2

sentences.zh: 安思閻皇后

translations.literal: Empress Yan, Ansi.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Yan of Emperor An (posthumously titled Ansi)

sentences.zh: 安思閻皇后諱姬，河南滎陽人也。

translations.literal: The Ansi Empress Yan had the taboo name Ji and was a native of Xingyang in Henan.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Yan Ji, honored after her death as Ansi, came from Xingyang in Henan.

sentences.zh: 祖父章，永平中為尚書，以二妹為貴人。

translations.literal: Her grandfather Zhang, during the Yongping era, was Minister of Writing, because his two younger sisters became noble ladies.
translations.idiomatic: Her grandfather Yan Zhang had served as Minister of Writing under Yongping; two of his younger daughters had entered the harem as noble ladies.

sentences.zh: 章精力曉舊典，久次，當遷以重職，顯宗為後宮親屬，竟不用，出為步兵校尉。

translations.literal: Zhang had vigor of spirit and understood old precedents; having long waited in rank, he ought to have been moved to a weighty post, but Emperor Ming, on account of rear-palace kin, ultimately did not employ him and sent him out as Colonel of the Infantry.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang was capable and steeped in statute and precedent; after long seniority he was due for a senior appointment, yet Emperor Ming, reluctant to empower his consorts’ kin, never gave him the post and instead sent him out as colonel of the infantry.

sentences.zh: 章生暢，暢生后。

translations.literal: Zhang begat Chang; Chang begat the empress.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang was father to Yan Chang, and Chang in turn was the empress’s father.

sentences.zh: 后有才色。

translations.literal: The empress had talent and beauty.
translations.idiomatic: She was gifted and strikingly beautiful.

sentences.zh: 元初元年，以選入掖庭，甚見寵愛，為貴人。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yuanchu, by selection she entered the Rear Palace and was greatly seen with favor and love, becoming a noble lady.
translations.idiomatic: In 114 she was chosen for the imperial harem, won the emperor’s deep affection, and was raised to noble lady.

sentences.zh: 二年，立為皇后。

translations.literal: In the second year, she was established as empress.
translations.idiomatic: The next year she was invested as empress.

sentences.zh: 后專房妒忌，帝幸宮人李氏，生皇子保，遂鴆殺李氏。

translations.literal: The empress monopolized the bedchamber and was jealous; the emperor favored the palace woman of the Li clan, who bore the imperial son Bao, and so she zhèn-poison-killed Lady Li.
translations.idiomatic: She dominated the emperor’s attentions and grew viciously jealous: when he lay with a chamber attendant surnamed Li who bore Prince Bao, the empress had Lady Li murdered with poisoned wine.

sentences.zh: 三年，以后父侍中暢為長水校尉，封北宜春侯，食邑五千戶。

translations.literal: In the third year, the empress’s father, Palace Attendant Chang, was made Colonel of the Long Waters and enfeoffed as Marquis of Northern Yichun with five thousand households of revenue.
translations.idiomatic: In 115 her father Yan Chang, a palace attendant, was appointed colonel of the Long Waters army and created marquis of Northern Yichun with a fief of five thousand households.

sentences.zh: 四年，暢卒，謚曰文侯，子顯嗣。

translations.literal: In the fourth year, Chang died; his posthumous title was Cultured Marquis, and his son Xian succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: The following year Yan Chang died and was posthumously titled the Cultured Marquis; his son Yan Xian inherited the marquisate.

sentences.zh: 建光元年，鄧太后崩，帝始親政事。

translations.literal: In the first year of Jianguang, Empress Dowager Deng died, and the emperor for the first time personally handled government affairs.
translations.idiomatic: When Empress Dowager Deng died in 121, Emperor An at last took the reins of government himself.

sentences.zh: 顯及弟景、耀、晏並為卿校，典禁兵。

translations.literal: Xian together with his younger brothers Jing, Yao, and Yan were all ministers and colonels and held charge of forbidden troops.
translations.idiomatic: Yan Xian and his brothers Jing, Yao, and Yan all held senior posts and colonelcies and commanded the palace guard.

sentences.zh: 延光元年，更封顯長社候，食邑萬三千五百戶，追尊后母宗為滎陽君。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yanguang, Xian was re-enfeoffed as Marquis of Changshe with revenue of thirteen thousand five hundred households, and the empress’s mother Zong was posthumously honored as Lady of Xingyang.
translations.idiomatic: In 122 Yan Xian was transferred to a marquisate at Changshe worth thirteen thousand five hundred households, and the empress dowager’s mother, née Zong, was given the noble title Lady of Xingyang.

sentences.zh: 顯、景諸子年皆童齔，並為黃門侍郎。

translations.literal: The sons of Xian and Jing were all of the age of losing milk teeth and together were Gentlemen-in-Attendance of the Yellow Gates.
translations.idiomatic: Even the sons of Yan Xian and Yan Jing—still little boys—were all appointed gentlemen at the Yellow Gates.

sentences.zh: 后寵既盛，而兄弟頗與朝權，后遂與大長秋江京、中常侍樊豐等共譖皇太子保，廢為濟陰王。

translations.literal: The empress’s favor having become great, and her brothers somewhat sharing court power, the empress then together with the Grand Prolonger of Autumn Jiang Jing, Regular Palace Attendant Fan Feng, and others jointly slandered the heir apparent Bao, who was deposed and made Prince of Jiyin.
translations.idiomatic: As her influence grew and her brothers muscled into state affairs, she joined Chief Steward Jiang Jing, eunuch Fan Feng, and their faction in framing Crown Prince Liu Bao; he was stripped of the succession and demoted to prince of Jiyin.

sentences.zh: 四年春，后從帝幸章陵，帝道疾，崩於葉縣。

translations.literal: In the spring of the fourth year, the empress followed the emperor in visiting Zhangling; on the road the emperor fell ill and died at Ye County.
translations.idiomatic: In the spring of 125 she accompanied the emperor on a visit to the imperial tombs at Zhangling; he took ill en route and died at Ye.

sentences.zh: 后、顯兄弟及江京、樊豐等謀曰：『今晏駕道次，濟陰王在內，邂逅公卿立之，還為大害。

translations.literal: The empress, Xian’s brothers, and Jiang Jing, Fan Feng, and others plotted, saying, ‘Now the imperial carriage has halted on the road; the Prince of Jiyin is inside the capital; if by chance the high ministers establish him, on return it will be a great harm.
translations.idiomatic: She and the Yan brothers, Jiang Jing, Fan Feng, and their circle conspired: “The sovereign has just died on the road; Liu Bao, the deposed heir, is still in the capital—if ministers seize the moment and put him on the throne, we are ruined.”

sentences.zh: 』乃偽云帝疾甚，徙御臥車。

translations.literal: ’ Then they falsely said the emperor’s illness was very severe and moved him to the imperial sleeping carriage.
translations.idiomatic: They spread word that the emperor was gravely ill and had him carried in the closed imperial litter.

sentences.zh: 行四日，驅馳還宮。

translations.literal: They traveled four days, galloping back to the palace.
translations.idiomatic: For four days they raced back to Luoyang without pausing for a proper lying-in-state.

sentences.zh: 明日，詐遣司徒劉熹詣郊廟社稷，告天請命。

translations.literal: The next day, they falsely sent Minister of Education Liu Xi to the suburban altars, the temples, and the altars of soil and grain to announce to Heaven and pray for mandate.
translations.idiomatic: The next morning they staged a mission: Grand Commandant Liu Xi was sent to the suburban and ancestral shrines to perform a bogus prayer for the emperor’s life.

sentences.zh: 其夕，乃發喪。

translations.literal: That evening, then they issued mourning.
translations.idiomatic: Only that evening did they announce his death.

sentences.zh: 尊后曰皇太后。

translations.literal: They honored the empress as empress dowager.
translations.idiomatic: She was proclaimed empress dowager.

sentences.zh: 皇太后臨朝，以顯為車騎將軍儀同三司。

translations.literal: The empress dowager faced the court; Xian was made General-in-Chief of Chariots and Cavalry with ceremonial parity of the Three Offices.
translations.idiomatic: She assumed the regency and appointed Yan Xian general-in-chief of chariots and cavalry with honors equal to the three highest ministers.

sentences.zh: 太后欲久專國政，貪立幼年，與顯等定策禁中，迎濟北惠王子北鄉侯懿，立為皇帝。

translations.literal: The empress dowager wished long to monopolize state government, coveting to establish someone young, and with Xian and others fixed policy inside the forbidden precincts, welcoming Yi, the Prince of Beixiang, son of Prince Hui of Jibei, and establishing him as emperor.
translations.idiomatic: Determined to prolong her grip on power, she wanted a child on the throne: inside the palace she and Yan Xian settled on Liu Yi, the young prince of Beixiang and grandson of Prince Hui of Jibei, and had him enthroned.

sentences.zh: 顯忌大將軍耿寶位尊權重，威行前朝，乃風有司奏寶及其黨與中常侍樊豐、虎賁中郎將謝惲、惲弟侍中篤、篤弟大將軍長史宓、侍中周廣、阿母野王君王聖、聖女永、永壻黃門侍郎樊嚴等，更相阿黨，互作威福，探刺禁省，更為唱和，皆大不道。

translations.literal: Xian envied Grand General Geng Bao’s position being honored and power being heavy, his awesomeness extending to the former court, and therefore he hinted to the responsible offices to memorialize Bao and his faction—Regular Palace Attendant Fan Feng, Gentleman of the Household for Rapid as a Tiger Xie Yun, Yun’s younger brother Xie Du as palace attendant, Du’s younger brother Mi as chief clerk to the grand general, Palace Attendant Zhou Guang, the nurse of Ye Wang Lady Wang Sheng, Sheng’s daughter Yong, Yong’s son-in-law Gentleman-in-Attendance of the Yellow Gates Fan Yan, and others—mutually forming a clique, mutually making awesomeness and blessing, spying into the forbidden inner palace, mutually becoming chorus and response, all greatly lacking in the Way.
translations.idiomatic: Yan Xian feared Grand General Geng Bao, whose prestige still overshadowed the old regime, and prompted officials to impeach Bao and his allies—Fan Feng, Xie Yun and his brother Xie Du, Du’s brother Mi as chief clerk to the grand general, Zhou Guang, the emperor’s wet nurse Wang Sheng of Yewang, her daughter Yong, Yong’s husband Fan Yan, and the rest—for forming a cabal, abusing power, eavesdropping on palace secrets, and covering for one another, charging them all with grave disloyalty.

sentences.zh: 豐、惲、廣皆下獄死，家屬徙比景；

translations.literal: Feng, Yun, and Guang were all cast into prison and died; their families were moved to Bijin;
translations.idiomatic: Fan Feng, Xie Yun, and Zhou Guang were jailed and executed; their kin were banished to the far south at Bijin;

sentences.zh: 宓、嚴減死，髡鉗；

translations.literal: Mi and Yan had death commuted, shaved heads and iron collars;
translations.idiomatic: Mi and Fan Yan were spared execution but sentenced to shave their heads and wear the cangue;

sentences.zh: 貶寶為則亭侯，遣就國，自殺；

translations.literal: Bao was demoted to Marquis of Zeting, sent to his state, and committed suicide;
translations.idiomatic: Geng Bao was stripped to a minor marquisate, ordered to his fief, and took his own life;

sentences.zh: 王聖母子徙雁門。

translations.literal: Wang Sheng and her son were moved to Yanmen.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Sheng and her son were exiled to Yanmen.

sentences.zh: 於是景為衛尉，耀城門校尉，晏執金吾，兄弟權要，威福自由。

translations.literal: Thereupon Jing became Commandant of the Guards, Yao Colonel of the City Gates, Yan Bearer of the Mace; the brothers were power-holders, awesomeness and blessing self-determined.
translations.idiomatic: Yan Jing was made commandant of the guards, Yan Yao colonel of the city gates, and Yan Yan bearer of the golden mace; the brothers now ran the court and did as they pleased.

sentences.zh: 少帝立二百餘日而疾篤，顯兄弟及江京等皆在左右。

translations.literal: The young emperor had been established more than two hundred days when his illness became grave; Xian’s brothers and Jiang Jing and others were all at his side.
translations.idiomatic: After little more than seven months on the throne the boy emperor lay dying; Yan Xian, his brothers, Jiang Jing, and their followers kept vigil at his bedside.

sentences.zh: 京引顯屏語曰：『北鄉侯病不解，國嗣宜時有定。

translations.literal: Jing drew Xian aside and spoke in secret, saying, ‘The Prince of Beixiang’s illness does not improve; the heir to the state ought soon to be fixed.
translations.idiomatic: Jiang Jing drew Yan Xian aside and whispered that the prince of Beixiang was failing and the succession had to be settled without delay.

sentences.zh: 前不用濟陰王，今若立之，後必當怨，又何不早徵諸王子，簡所置乎？

translations.literal: Formerly we did not use the Prince of Jiyin; if now we establish him, afterward he will surely resent us—why not early summon the various imperial sons and select whom to place?
translations.idiomatic: They had passed over Liu Bao once; if he were enthroned now he would surely take revenge—why not summon eligible princes from the provinces at once and choose someone they could control?

sentences.zh: 』顯以為然。

translations.literal: ’ Xian deemed it so.
translations.idiomatic: Yan Xian agreed.

sentences.zh: 及少帝薨，京白太后，徵濟北、河間王子。

translations.literal: When the young emperor died, Jing reported to the empress dowager and summoned the sons of the princes of Jibei and Hejian.
translations.idiomatic: When the child emperor died, Jiang Jing informed the empress dowager and issued summonses for young princes from Jibei and Hejian.

sentences.zh: 未至，而中黃門孫程合謀殺江京等，立濟陰王，是為順帝。

translations.literal: Before they arrived, Attendant at the Yellow Gates of the Center Sun Cheng joined in a plot to kill Jiang Jing and others and establish the Prince of Jiyin, who is Emperor Shun.
translations.idiomatic: Before those boys reached the capital, the eunuch Sun Cheng and his confederates struck, killed Jiang Jing and his faction, and enthroned Liu Bao of Jiyin as Emperor Shun.

sentences.zh: 顯、景、晏及黨與皆伏誅，遷太后於離宮，家屬徙比景。

translations.literal: Xian, Jing, Yan, and their faction were all executed; the empress dowager was moved to a detached palace; families were moved to Bijin.
translations.idiomatic: Yan Xian, Yan Jing, Yan Yan, and their accomplices were put to death; the empress dowager was confined to a secondary palace; and their relatives were banished to Bijin.

sentences.zh: 明年，太后崩。

translations.literal: The next year, the empress dowager died.
translations.idiomatic: She died the following year.

sentences.zh: 在位十二年，合葬恭陵。

translations.literal: Having held position twelve years, she was jointly buried at Gong Tomb.
translations.idiomatic: She had reigned as empress or empress dowager for twelve years and was buried with Emperor An at the Gong mausoleum.

sentences.zh: 帝母李氏瘞在洛陽城北，帝初不知，莫敢以聞。

translations.literal: The emperor’s mother Lady Li was buried north of Luoyang; the emperor at first did not know, and none dared to report it.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Shun’s mother, Lady Li, had been given a secret grave north of the city; he grew up unaware of it, for no one dared tell him.

sentences.zh: 及太后崩，左右白之，帝感悟發哀，親至瘞所，更以禮殯，上尊謚曰恭愍皇后，葬恭北陵，為策書金匱，藏於世祖廟。

translations.literal: When the empress dowager died, those at his side informed him; the emperor was moved to insight and issued mourning, personally went to the burial place, again buried her with rites, elevated her honorable posthumous title as Empress Gongmin, buried her at Gong North Tomb, made a document in a metal coffer, and stored it in Shizu’s temple.
translations.idiomatic: After the empress dowager’s death his attendants finally spoke; stricken with grief, he visited the site, gave her a proper state funeral, and posthumously titled her Empress Gongmin (“respectful and pitied”); she was interred at the northern adjunct of the Gong mausoleum, and the patent of ennoblement was sealed in a golden casket in Emperor Guangwu’s temple.

sentences.zh: 順烈梁皇后

translations.literal: Empress Liang the Shunlie.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Liang of Emperor Shun (posthumously titled Shunlie, “compliant and ardent”)

sentences.zh: 順烈梁皇后諱妠，大將軍商之女，恭懷皇后弟之孫也。

translations.literal: The Shunlie Empress Liang had the taboo name Na; she was the daughter of Grand General Shang and a granddaughter of Empress Gonghuai’s younger brother.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Liang Na, honored after death as Shunlie, was the daughter of Grand General Liang Shang and a granddaughter of Empress Gonghuai’s younger brother (Liang Na was thus of the same Liang clan line that had produced an earlier empress).

sentences.zh: 后生，有光景之祥。

translations.literal: When the empress was born, there was an auspicious sign of light and radiance.
translations.idiomatic: Her birth was said to have been marked by an unearthly glow.

sentences.zh: 少善女工。

translations.literal: In youth she was good at women’s work.
translations.idiomatic: As a girl she excelled at needlework and weaving.

sentences.zh: 好《史書》，九歲能誦《論語》，治《韓詩》，大義略舉。

translations.literal: She loved the clerical script; at nine she could recite the Analects; she studied the Han version of the Odes and broadly grasped the great meanings.
translations.idiomatic: She loved calligraphy in clerical script, had the Analects by heart at nine, and studied the Han-school Book of Odes until she had mastered its main themes.

sentences.zh: 常以列女圖畫置於左右，以自監戒。

translations.literal: She constantly placed illustrations of exemplary women at her left and right to supervise and warn herself.
translations.idiomatic: She kept portraits of exemplary women always in view as a private mirror for her conduct.

sentences.zh: 父商深異之，竊謂諸弟曰：『我先人全濟河西，所活者不可勝數。

translations.literal: Her father Shang deeply marveled at her and secretly said to his younger brothers, ‘Our forebears wholly saved and aided west of the River; those they kept alive cannot be counted.
translations.idiomatic: Her father Liang Shang marveled at her and once said privately to his brothers, “Our forebears brought relief west of the Yellow River and saved more lives than anyone could count.

sentences.zh: 雖大位不究，而積德必報。

translations.literal: Although the great position was not fully realized, accumulated virtue must receive recompense.
translations.idiomatic: They never reached the highest offices themselves, yet stored-up virtue is always rewarded in time.

sentences.zh: 若慶流子孫者，倘興此女乎？』

translations.literal: If blessing flows to descendants, might it rise in this girl?’
translations.idiomatic: If fortune is to pass to our descendants, perhaps it will begin with this girl.”

sentences.zh: 永建三年，與姑俱選入掖庭，時年十三，相工茅通見后，驚，再拜賀曰：『此所謂日角偃月，相之極貴，臣所未嘗見也。

translations.literal: In the third year of Yongjian, together with her aunt she was selected into the Rear Palace; at the time she was thirteen; physiognomist Mao Tong saw the empress, was startled, bowed twice and congratulated, saying, ‘This is what is called sun-horn and reclining moon, the utmost nobility of physiognomy; your servant has never seen it.
translations.idiomatic: In 128 she entered the harem with an aunt, aged thirteen; the face-reader Mao Tong took one look, bowed twice, and exclaimed, “The sun-horn and crescent-moon pattern—the mark of supreme eminence. I have never seen its like.”

sentences.zh: 』太史卜兆得壽房，又筮得《坤》之《比》，遂以為貴人。

translations.literal: ’ The Grand Clerk divined the site and obtained Shoufang; again they milfoiled and obtained Kun changing to Bi; thereupon she was made a noble lady.
translations.idiomatic: The court astrologers divined an auspicious burial site at Shoufang and cast the stalks to Kun turning into Bi—both highly favorable—so she was raised to noble lady.

sentences.zh: 常特被引御，從容辭於帝曰：『夫陽以博施為德，陰以不專為義，螽斯則百，福之所由興也。

translations.literal: She was often specially summoned to imperial attendance and calmly declined to the emperor, saying, ‘Now yang takes broad bestowal as virtue, yin takes not monopoly as righteousness; the katydids then are a hundred—this is where blessing arises.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor often singled her out for his bed; she spoke to him with quiet firmness: “In nature the male principle spreads its favor widely; the female principle does not hoard exclusivity—that is how the Odes praise many heirs and how fortune grows.

sentences.zh: 願陛下思雲雨之均澤，識貫魚之次序，使小妾得免罪謗之累。

translations.literal: I wish Your Majesty will ponder the even moisture of cloud and rain, recognize the order of fish strung on a cord, and cause your petty concubine to obtain escape from the burden of crime and slander.
translations.idiomatic: I beg you to remember that rain should fall on all alike and that consorts have their proper order, so that your humble servant may be spared the envy and reproach that follow monopoly.

sentences.zh: 』由是帝加敬焉。

translations.literal: ’ From this the emperor increased respect for her.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor respected her all the more for it.

sentences.zh: 陽嘉元年春，有司奏立長秋宮，以乘氏侯商先帝外戚，《春秋》之義，娶先大國，梁小貴人宜配天祚，正位坤極。

translations.literal: In the spring of the first year of Yangjia, the responsible offices memorialized to establish the Palace of Long Autumn; because Marquis of Chengsi Shang was a former emperor’s outer kin, by the meaning of the Spring and Autumn one takes first a great state; the Liang lesser noble lady ought to match Heaven’s blessing and rectify position at the utmost of Earth.
translations.idiomatic: In the spring of 132 officials petitioned to fill the empress’s seat: by Spring and Autumn precedent a ruler should marry from a great house, and Lady Liang—daughter of the Marquis of Chengsi and Liang Shang, a maternal relative of the late emperor—was fit to receive Heaven’s mandate as mistress of the inner palace.

sentences.zh: 帝從之，乃於壽安殿立貴人為皇后。

translations.literal: The emperor followed it; then at Shou’an Hall he established the noble lady as empress.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Shun agreed and, in the Hall of Longevity and Peace, invested her as empress.

sentences.zh: 后既少聰惠，深覽前世得失，雖以德進，不敢有驕專之心，每日月見讁，輒降服求愆。

translations.literal: The empress, being from youth intelligent and keen, deeply surveyed former generations’ gain and loss; although she advanced by virtue, she did not dare have a proud monopolizing heart; whenever the sun and moon met with reproach (eclipses), then she lowered garments seeking fault.
translations.idiomatic: Clever from girlhood and steeped in the lessons of history, she never let virtue go to her head: whenever the court almanac recorded an eclipse or other ominous sign in sun or moon, she would lay aside her finery and ask whether she had offended Heaven.

sentences.zh: 建康元年，帝崩。

translations.literal: In the first year of Jiankang, the emperor died.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Shun died in 144.

sentences.zh: 后無子，美人虞氏子炳立，是為沖帝。

translations.literal: The empress had no son; the son of the Beauty of the Yu clan, Bing, was established; this is Emperor Chong.
translations.idiomatic: She had borne no heir, so Liu Bing, son of Lady Yu the Beauty of the Bedchamber, succeeded as Emperor Chong.

sentences.zh: 尊后為皇太后，太后臨朝。

translations.literal: They honored the empress as empress dowager; the empress dowager faced the court.
translations.idiomatic: She was named empress dowager and assumed the regency.

sentences.zh: 沖帝尋崩，復立質帝，猶秉朝政。

translations.literal: Emperor Chong soon died; they again established Emperor Zhi; she still grasped court government.
translations.idiomatic: When Chong died within months, she enthroned Emperor Zhi and kept hold of the government.

sentences.zh: 時，楊、徐劇賊寇擾州郡，西羌、鮮卑及日南蠻夷攻城暴掠，賦斂煩數，官民困竭。

translations.literal: At the time, fierce bandits of Yang and Xu raided and disturbed prefectures and commanderies; Western Qiang, Xianbei, and the southern barbarians of Rinan attacked cities and violently plundered; levies were frequent and numerous; officials and commoners were exhausted.
translations.idiomatic: The age was one of turmoil: bandit armies ravaged Yang and Xu, Qiang and Xianbei raiders and tribesmen from Rinan stormed towns, and relentless taxation left officials and people alike destitute.

sentences.zh: 太后夙夜勤勞，推心杖賢，委任太尉李固等，拔用忠良，務崇節儉。

translations.literal: The empress dowager toiled diligently morning and night, pushing her heart and leaning on worthies, entrusting Grand Commandant Li Gu and others, elevating the loyal and good, striving to honor frugality.
translations.idiomatic: The empress dowager rose early and worked late, put her trust in able men, gave Grand Commandant Li Gu and other loyal ministers real authority, promoted the upright, and pressed for austerity at court.

sentences.zh: 其貪叨罪慝，多見誅廢。

translations.literal: Those who were greedy and grasping in crime and evil were mostly seen executed or dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: The corrupt and vicious were purged or cashiered in large numbers.

sentences.zh: 分兵討伐，群寇消夷。

translations.literal: They divided troops to attack and punish; the mass of bandits was extinguished.
translations.idiomatic: Troops were sent on several fronts until the rebel hosts were broken.

sentences.zh: 故海內肅然，宗廟以寧。

translations.literal: Therefore within the seas there was solemn order; the ancestral temple thereby was tranquil.
translations.idiomatic: For a time the realm grew quiet again and the imperial house seemed secure.

sentences.zh: 而兄大將軍冀鴆殺質帝，專權暴濫，忌害忠良，數以邪說疑誤太后，遂立桓帝而誅李固。

translations.literal: But her elder brother Grand General Ji poisoned Emperor Zhi, monopolized power, was violent and excessive, envied and harmed the loyal and good, repeatedly by deviant doctrines misled the empress dowager, thereupon established Emperor Huan and executed Li Gu.
translations.idiomatic: Then her brother, Grand General Liang Ji, poisoned Emperor Zhi, seized unchecked power, persecuted good men, fed the empress dowager a stream of lies, set Emperor Huan on the throne, and had Li Gu put to death.

sentences.zh: 太后又溺於宦官，多所封寵，以此天下失望。

translations.literal: The empress dowager again drowned in palace attendants, many were enfeoffed and favored; by this the empire was disappointed.
translations.idiomatic: She also indulged the eunuchs with lavish enfeoffments, and the empire’s hopes in her rule faded.

sentences.zh: 和平元年春，歸政於帝，太后寢疾遂篤，乃御輦幸宣德殿，見宮省官屬及諸梁兄弟。

translations.literal: In the spring of the first year of Heping, she returned government to the emperor; the empress dowager’s lying illness then became grave; then she rode the imperial carriage and visited Xuande Hall, seeing palace and ministry officials and the various Liang brothers.
translations.idiomatic: In the spring of 150 she formally handed power back to Emperor Huan; as her illness worsened she was carried by litter to Xuande Hall for a last audience with palace staff and the men of the Liang clan.

sentences.zh: 詔曰：『朕素有心下結氣，從間以來，加以浮腫，逆害飲食，寖以沈困，比使內外勞心請禱。

translations.literal: An edict said, ‘We have always had knot-breath below the heart; since the remission, added to it swelling, contrary harm to drink and food, gradually sinking into exhaustion; lately causing those within and without to labor the heart with prayers.
translations.idiomatic: She issued an edict: for years she had suffered from a painful knot below the heart; recently edema had set in, she could not keep food down, and she grew weaker by the day, while the whole court wore itself out in prayer on her behalf.

sentences.zh: 私自忖度，日夜虛劣，不能復與群公卿士共相終竟。

translations.literal: Privately We reckon that day and night We are empty and weak; unable again with the host of dukes, ministers, and gentlemen to carry things through to the end together.
translations.idiomatic: She felt herself ebbing hour by hour and knew she would not finish what they had begun together with the ministers at her side.

sentences.zh: 援立聖嗣，恨不久育養，見其終始。

translations.literal: In aiding and establishing the holy heir, We regret not being able long to nurture and rear him, to see his beginning and end.
translations.idiomatic: She had set the present emperor on the throne yet would not live long enough to guide him from youth to full maturity.

sentences.zh: 今以皇帝、將軍兄弟委付股肱，其各自勉焉。

translations.literal: Now We entrust the emperor and the general’s younger brothers to the thigh and forearm; each of you exert yourselves.
translations.idiomatic: She commended the emperor and the general’s brothers to them as her chief ministers, urging each to strive not to fail those charges.

sentences.zh: 』後二日而崩。

translations.literal: ’ After two days then she died.
translations.idiomatic: Two days later she was gone.

sentences.zh: 在位十九年，年四十五。

translations.literal: Having held position nineteen years, age forty-five.
translations.idiomatic: She had held empress or empress dowager rank for nineteen years and died at forty-five.

sentences.zh: 合葬憲陵。

translations.literal: Jointly buried at Xian Tomb.
translations.idiomatic: She was buried with Emperor Shun at the Xian mausoleum.

translations.literal: 〈Appendix〉
translations.idiomatic: Supplement

sentences.zh: 虞美人、陳夫人

translations.literal: The Beauty Yu, Lady Chen.
translations.idiomatic: Lady Yu the Beauty and Lady Chen

sentences.zh: 虞美人者，以良家子年十三選入掖庭，又生女舞陽長公主。

translations.literal: As for the Beauty Yu, by good-family daughter at age thirteen she was selected into the Rear Palace; moreover she bore a daughter, the Princess of Wuyang.
translations.idiomatic: Lady Yu the Beauty had entered the harem at thirteen as a girl from a respectable family and later bore a daughter who became the senior princess of Wuyang.

sentences.zh: 自漢興，母氏莫不尊寵。

translations.literal: Since Han rose, mothers’ clans have none not been honored and favored.
translations.idiomatic: Since the founding of Han, mothers of emperors had invariably been honored.

sentences.zh: 順帝既未加美人爵號，而沖帝早夭，大將軍梁冀秉政，忌惡佗族，故虞氏抑而不登，但稱『大家』而已。

translations.literal: Emperor Shun had not added the beauty’s title and rank; and Emperor Chong died young; Grand General Liang Ji grasped government, envied and hated other clans; therefore the Yu clan was suppressed and not elevated, only called ‘Great One’ and that was all.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Shun had never raised her to a titled consort; Emperor Chong died in infancy; and under Grand General Liang Ji, who loathed any rival kin, the Yu family was kept down—she was known only by the informal honorific “madam” (dajia).

sentences.zh: 陳夫人者，家本魏郡，少以聲伎入孝王宮，得幸，生質帝。

translations.literal: As for Lady Chen, her family was originally in Wei commandery; in youth by voice and skill she entered Prince Xiao’s palace, obtained favor, and bore Emperor Zhi.
translations.idiomatic: Lady Chen came from Wei commandery; as a girl she had joined Prince Xiao’s household as a singer and entertainer, won his favor, and bore Emperor Zhi.

sentences.zh: 亦以梁氏故，榮寵不及焉。

translations.literal: Also because of the Liang clan, glory and favor did not reach her.
translations.idiomatic: For the same reason—the Liang stranglehold on the court—she too received no honors.

sentences.zh: 熹平四年，小黃門趙祐、議郎卑整上言：『《春秋》之義，母以子貴。

translations.literal: In the fourth year of Xiping, Junior Attendant at the Yellow Gates Zhao You and Consultant Bei Zheng submitted words, saying, ‘By the meaning of the Spring and Autumn, the mother is honored by the son.
translations.idiomatic: In 175 the junior eunuch Zhao You and the consultant Bei Zheng memorialized that the Spring and Autumn teaches a mother’s rank to rise with her son.

sentences.zh: 隆漢盛典，尊崇母氏，凡在外戚，莫不加寵。

translations.literal: In Han’s splendid codes, honoring mother’s clans—every outer kin—none was not increased in favor.
translations.idiomatic: Han’s grand precedents exalted imperial mothers and showered favor on every branch of maternal kin.

sentences.zh: 今沖帝母虞大家，質帝母陳夫人，皆誕生聖皇，而未有稱號。

translations.literal: Now Emperor Chong’s mother the Yu Great One and Emperor Zhi’s mother Lady Chen both bore sagely sovereigns, yet have no titles.
translations.idiomatic: Yet Emperor Chong’s mother, Lady Yu, and Emperor Zhi’s mother, Lady Chen, who each bore a Son of Heaven, still lack formal titles.

sentences.zh: 夫臣子雖賤，尚有追贈之典，況二母見在，不蒙崇顯之次，無以述遵先世，垂示後世也。

translations.literal: Now though ministers and sons are lowly, still there are canons of posthumous enfeoffment; how much more the two mothers are still alive and do not receive the ranks of honor and display—there is no means to narrate compliance with former generations or hang down display to later generations.
translations.idiomatic: Even humble subjects may be honored after death; how much more two living mothers of emperors? Leaving them without rank fails both our ancestors’ example and the lesson we owe posterity.

sentences.zh: 』帝感其言，乃拜虞大家為憲陵貴人，陳夫人為渤海孝王妃，使中常侍持節授印綬，遣太常以三牲告憲陵、懷陵、靜陵焉。

translations.literal: ’ The emperor was moved by their words; then he appointed the Yu Great One as Noble Lady of Xianling, Lady Chen as consort to Prince Xiao of Bohai, sent a Regular Palace Attendant bearing staff of authority to confer seals and ribbons, and dispatched the Grand Master of Ceremonies with three sacrificial animals to announce at Xianling, Huailing, and Jingling.
translations.idiomatic: Moved by this, Emperor Ling created Lady Yu noble lady of the Xian mausoleum (Emperor Shun’s tomb) and titled Lady Chen consort to the late Prince Xiao of Bohai; a eunuch minister carried the imperial staff to invest them, and the Grand Master of Ceremonies offered sacrifice at the three imperial tombs to report the act.

sentences.zh: 孝崇匽皇后

translations.literal: Empress Yan, Xiaochong.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Yan of Emperor Huan (posthumously titled Xiaochong)

sentences.zh: 懿獻梁皇后

translations.literal: Empress Liang the Yixian.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Liang of Emperor Huan (posthumously titled Yixian, “kind and worthy”)

sentences.zh: 桓帝懿獻梁皇后諱女瑩，順烈皇后之女弟也。

translations.literal: Emperor Huan’s Yixian Empress Liang had the taboo name Nüying; she was the Shunlie Empress’s younger sister.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Liang Nüying, honored after death as Yixian, was the younger sister of Empress Liang Na (Shunlie).

sentences.zh: 帝初為蠡吾侯，梁太后徵，欲與后為婚，未及嘉禮，會質帝崩，因以立帝。

translations.literal: The emperor at first was Marquis of Liwu; Empress Dowager Liang summoned him, wishing to make marriage with the empress; before reaching the auspicious rites, it happened that Emperor Zhi died; thereby he was established emperor.
translations.idiomatic: While Liu Zhi was still only marquis of Liwu, Empress Dowager Liang had summoned him to the capital intending to betroth him to her sister; before the wedding could be held Emperor Zhi died, and the Liang faction used the moment to enthrone him as Emperor Huan.

sentences.zh: 明年，有司奏太后曰：『《春秋》迎王后于紀，在塗則稱后。

translations.literal: The next year, the responsible offices memorialized to the empress dowager, saying, ‘The Spring and Autumn: welcoming the king’s consort at Ji—on the road then she is called consort.
translations.idiomatic: The following year officials told the empress dowager: “The Spring and Autumn records that when a king’s bride was brought from the state of Ji, she was already addressed as queen while still on the journey.”

sentences.zh: 今大將軍冀女弟，膺紹聖善。

translations.literal: Now Grand General Ji’s younger sister has received and continued sagely goodness.
translations.idiomatic: The grand general’s younger sister is of proven virtue and fit to continue the sacred line.”

sentences.zh: 結婚之際，有命既集，宜備禮章，時進徵幣。

translations.literal: At the juncture of tying marriage, the mandate having already gathered, it is fitting to complete ritual regulations and in season advance betrothal goods.
translations.idiomatic: The match is decreed; the full betrothal rites should be observed and the bride-price sent without delay.”

sentences.zh: 請下三公、太常案禮儀。

translations.literal: We request sending down to the Three Excellencies and the Grand Master of Ceremonies to examine ritual precedents.
translations.idiomatic: We ask that the Three Excellencies and the Grand Master of Ceremonies be ordered to review the canonical forms.”

sentences.zh: 』奏可。

translations.literal: ’ The memorial was approved.
translations.idiomatic: The memorial was approved.

sentences.zh: 於是悉依孝惠皇帝納后故事，聘黃金二萬斤，納采鴈、璧、乘馬、束帛，一如舊典。

translations.literal: Thereupon they wholly followed Emperor Xiaohui’s story of taking a consort: betrothal of twenty thousand jin of gold, presentation of geese, jade disks, team horses, and bundled silks, all according to old canons.
translations.idiomatic: Everything then followed the precedent of Emperor Hui’s empress-taking: twenty thousand jin of gold as bride-price, with geese, bi jades, a four-horse team, and silks for the na cai ceremony, exactly as the old statutes prescribed.

sentences.zh: 建和元年六月始入掖庭，八月立為皇后。

translations.literal: In the sixth month of the first year of Jianhe she first entered the Rear Palace; in the eighth month she was established as empress.
translations.idiomatic: She entered the harem in the sixth month of 147 and was crowned empress that August.

sentences.zh: 時，太后秉政而梁冀專朝，故后獨得寵幸，自下莫得進見。

translations.literal: At the time the empress dowager grasped government and Liang Ji monopolized the court, therefore the empress alone obtained favor and love; from below none could advance to audience.
translations.idiomatic: With Empress Dowager Liang regent and Liang Ji running the court, the new empress alone held the emperor’s heart; no other woman could reach him.

sentences.zh: 后藉姊兄廕勢，恣極奢靡，宮幄彫麗，服御珍華，巧飾制度，兼倍前世。

translations.literal: The empress relied on elder sister and elder brother’s shade power, indulging to the utmost luxury; palace curtains were carved and splendid; dress and equipage were precious and brilliant; cleverly ornamented regulations doubled those of former generations.
translations.idiomatic: Backed by her sister and brother, she spent without limit: chambers hung with carved silks, gowns and gems rivaling an emperor’s, and every mark of rank contrived to outshine any earlier empress twice over.

sentences.zh: 及皇太后崩，恩愛稍衰。

translations.literal: When the empress dowager died, favor and love gradually faded.
translations.idiomatic: After the empress dowager died, the emperor’s ardor cooled.

sentences.zh: 后既無子，潛懷怨忌，每宮人孕育，鮮得全者。

translations.literal: The empress having no son, secretly harbored resentment and jealousy; whenever palace women conceived, few obtained preservation intact.
translations.idiomatic: Childless and secretly vicious, she saw to it that few pregnant concubines bore a living child.

sentences.zh: 帝雖迫畏梁冀，不敢譴怒，然見御轉稀。

translations.literal: Although the emperor was compelled to fear Liang Ji and did not dare rebuke in anger, yet audiences became progressively sparse.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Huan still dared not defy Liang Ji openly, but he visited her bedchamber less and less.

sentences.zh: 至延熹二年，后以憂恚崩，在位十三年，葬懿陵。

translations.literal: Reaching the second year of Yanxi, the empress died of grief and resentment; having held position thirteen years, she was buried at Yi Tomb.
translations.idiomatic: In 159 she died, broken by anxiety and spite, after thirteen years as empress, and was buried at the Yi mausoleum.

sentences.zh: 其歲，誅梁冀，廢懿陵為貴人冢焉。

translations.literal: That year Liang Ji was executed; Yi Tomb was abolished and made a noble lady’s mound.
translations.idiomatic: The same year Liang Ji was executed and her grand tomb was reduced to the scale proper for a concubine’s grave.

sentences.zh: 桓帝鄧皇后

translations.literal: Empress Deng of Emperor Huan.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Deng of Emperor Huan

sentences.zh: 桓帝鄧皇后諱猛女，和熹皇后從兄子鄧香之女也。

translations.literal: Empress Deng of Emperor Huan had the taboo name Mengnü; she was the daughter of Deng Xiang, son of a cousin on the elder-brother line of Empress Hexi.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Deng Mengnü was the daughter of Deng Xiang, a nephew of the great Empress Deng Sui (Hexi) on the male line.

sentences.zh: 母宣，初適香，生后。

translations.literal: Her mother Xuan, at first married to Xiang, bore the empress.
translations.idiomatic: Her mother, née Xuan, had first been married to Deng Xiang and bore her there.

sentences.zh: 改嫁梁紀，紀者，大將軍梁冀妻孫壽之舅也。

translations.literal: She remarried Liang Ji; as for Ji, he was the maternal uncle of Grand General Liang Ji’s wife Sun Shou.
translations.idiomatic: Xuan then remarried a different man also named Liang Ji, not the grand general; he was the maternal uncle of Sun Shou, the grand general Liang Ji’s wife.

sentences.zh: 后少孤，隨母為居，因冒姓梁氏。

translations.literal: The empress in youth was orphaned; she followed her mother in residence and therefore falsely took the surname Liang.
translations.idiomatic: The girl was left fatherless early and lived with her mother, passing herself off under the Liang surname.

sentences.zh: 冀妻見后貌美，永興中進入掖庭，為采女，絕幸。

translations.literal: Ji’s wife saw the empress’s beauty was great; in Yongxing she was advanced into the Rear Palace, becoming a chosen woman, with extreme favor.
translations.idiomatic: Sun Shou, struck by her looks, had her sent to the harem during Yongxing as a low-ranking chosen girl; the emperor favored her above all others.

sentences.zh: 明年，封兄鄧演為南頓侯，位特進。

translations.literal: The next year, her elder brother Deng Yan was enfeoffed as Marquis of Nandun, rank specially advanced.
translations.idiomatic: The next year her brother Deng Yan was created marquis of Nandun with the honorary rank of specially advanced.

sentences.zh: 演卒，子康嗣。

translations.literal: Yan died; his son Kang succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: When Yan died, his son Kang inherited the title.

sentences.zh: 及懿獻后崩，梁冀誅，立后為皇后。

translations.literal: When the Yixian empress died, Liang Ji was executed, and the empress was established as empress.
translations.idiomatic: After Empress Liang Nüying died and Liang Ji was put to death, Deng Mengnü was raised to empress.

sentences.zh: 帝惡梁氏，改姓為薄，封后母宣為長安君。

translations.literal: The emperor hated the Liang clan; he changed the surname to Bo and enfeoffed the empress’s mother Xuan as Lady of Chang’an.
translations.idiomatic: To erase the taint of the Liangs he had her registered as Bo instead of Liang and created Lady Xuan noblewoman of Chang’an.

sentences.zh: 四年，有司奏后本郎中鄧香之女，不宜改易它姓，於是復為鄧氏。

translations.literal: In the fourth year, the responsible offices memorialized that the empress was originally the daughter of Gentleman Deng Xiang; it was not fitting to change to another surname; thereupon she again became the Deng clan.
translations.idiomatic: In 161 officials pointed out that she was truly the daughter of Deng Xiang, a court gentleman, and that the false surname should be dropped; she was restored as Deng.

sentences.zh: 追封贈香車騎將軍安陽侯印綬，更封宣、康大縣，宣為昆陽君，康為沘陽侯，賞賜巨萬計。

translations.literal: They posthumously enfeoffed and gifted Xiang General of Chariots and Cavalry Marquis of Anyang seals and ribbons; again they enfeoffed Xuan and Kang with great counties—Xuan as Lady of Kunyang, Kang as Marquis of Biyang—rewards and gifts reckoned in myriads of cash.
translations.idiomatic: Her dead father Xiang was posthumously titled general of chariots and cavalry and marquis of Anyang; Lady Xuan and Kang received rich new fiefs—she as lady of Kunyang, he as marquis of Biyang—along with gifts worth untold millions.

sentences.zh: 宣卒，賵贈葬禮，皆依后母舊儀。

translations.literal: When Xuan died, condolence gifts and burial rites all followed the old precedents for an empress’s mother.
translations.idiomatic: When Lady Xuan died, her obsequies matched those laid down for an empress dowager’s mother.

sentences.zh: 以康弟統襲封昆陽侯，位侍中；

translations.literal: They made Kang’s younger brother Tong succeed to the enfeoffment as Marquis of Kunyang, rank Palace Attendant;
translations.idiomatic: Kang’s brother Tong inherited the Kunyang marquisate and was appointed palace attendant;

sentences.zh: 統從兄會襲安陽侯，為虎賁中郎將；

translations.literal: Tong’s elder male cousin Hui succeeded to the Marquis of Anyang, becoming Gentleman of the Household for Rapid as a Tiger;
translations.idiomatic: Tong’s cousin Hui took over the Anyang title as gentleman of the household for rapid as a tiger;

sentences.zh: 又封統弟秉為淯陽侯。

translations.literal: Again they enfeoffed Tong’s younger brother Bing as Marquis of Yuyang.
translations.idiomatic: and Tong’s younger brother Bing was made marquis of Yuyang.

sentences.zh: 宗族皆列校、郎將。

translations.literal: The whole kin group were all arrayed as colonels and generals.
translations.idiomatic: The whole Deng kindred was packed into colonelcies and generalships.

sentences.zh: 帝多內幸，博采宮女至五六千人，及驅役從使，復兼倍於此。

translations.literal: The emperor had many inner favorites, broadly gathering palace women to five or six thousand, and as for driven labor and attendant runners, again doubled on top of this.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor kept a vast harem—five or six thousand palace women—and more than twice as many servants and attendants to wait on them.

sentences.zh: 而后恃尊驕忌，與帝所幸郭貴人更相譖訴。

translations.literal: But the empress relied on honor, was proud and jealous, and with the Noble Lady Guo whom the emperor favored mutually slandered and accused.
translations.idiomatic: The empress grew arrogant and jealous in her rank and traded accusations with Lady Guo, the emperor’s favorite.

sentences.zh: 八年，詔廢后，送暴室，以憂死。

translations.literal: In the eighth year, an edict deposed the empress; she was sent to the Blazing Room and died of grief.
translations.idiomatic: In 165 she was deposed by edict, confined to the palace dye-works prison, and died of grief there.

sentences.zh: 立七年。

translations.literal: Having been established seven years.
translations.idiomatic: She had been empress for seven years.

sentences.zh: 葬於北邙。

translations.literal: Buried at North Mang.
translations.idiomatic: She was buried on Mount Mang north of Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 從父河南尹萬世及會皆下獄死。

translations.literal: Her father’s male cousin Deng Wanshi, Administrator of Henan, and Hui were both cast into prison and died.
translations.idiomatic: Her kinsman Deng Wanshi, governor of Henan, and Deng Hui were jailed and died.

sentences.zh: 統等亦繫暴室，免官爵，歸本郡，財物沒入縣官。

translations.literal: Tong and others were also tied in the Blazing Room, stripped of office and rank, returned to their native commanderies, goods and wealth confiscated into the exchequer.
translations.idiomatic: Tong and his brothers were imprisoned too, stripped of rank, sent home to their commanderies, and their property seized for the treasury.

sentences.zh: 桓思竇皇后

translations.literal: Empress Dou the Huansi.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Dou of Emperor Huan (posthumously titled Huansi)

sentences.zh: 桓思竇皇后諱妙，章德皇后從祖弟之孫女也。

translations.literal: Empress Dou the Huansi had the taboo name Miao; she was a granddaughter on the line of Empress Zhangde’s cousin once removed on the younger-brother side.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Dou Miao, honored after death as Huansi, was a granddaughter of a male cousin of Empress Dou the Zhangde (and-Empress Dowager).

sentences.zh: 父武。

translations.literal: Her father was Wu.
translations.idiomatic: Her father was Dou Wu.

sentences.zh: 延熹八年，鄧皇后廢，后以選入掖庭為貴人，其冬，立為皇后，而御見甚稀，帝所寵唯采女田聖等。

translations.literal: In the eighth year of Yanxi, Empress Deng was deposed; the empress by selection entered the Rear Palace as a noble lady; that winter she was established as empress, but imperial audiences were very sparse; those the emperor favored were only chosen women such as Tian Sheng.
translations.idiomatic: When Empress Deng fell in 165, Dou Miao entered the harem as a noble lady and was made empress that winter, yet the emperor rarely came to her; his heart belonged to chosen girls like Tian Sheng.

sentences.zh: 永康元年冬，帝寢疾，遂以聖等九女皆為貴人。

translations.literal: In the winter of the first year of Yongkang, the emperor lay ill in bed; thereupon Sheng and nine women were all made noble ladies.
translations.idiomatic: In the winter of 167, as Emperor Huan lay dying, he raised Tian Sheng and eight other favorites to noble lady in a single stroke.

sentences.zh: 及崩，無嗣，后為皇太后。

translations.literal: When he died, there was no heir; the empress became empress dowager.
translations.idiomatic: He left no son; Dou Miao became empress dowager.

sentences.zh: 太后臨朝定策，立解犢亭侯宏，是為靈帝。

translations.literal: The empress dowager faced the court and fixed policy, establishing Hong, Marquis of Jieduting, who is Emperor Ling.
translations.idiomatic: She took the regency, chose Liu Hong, marquis of Jieduting, as heir, and enthroned him as Emperor Ling.

sentences.zh: 太后素忌忍，積怒田聖等，桓帝梓宮尚在前殿，遂殺田聖。

translations.literal: The empress dowager was by nature jealous and cruel; she had long accumulated anger toward Tian Sheng and others; Emperor Huan’s coffin was still in the front hall; then she killed Tian Sheng.
translations.idiomatic: Jealous and ruthless by nature, she had long hated Tian Sheng; while Emperor Huan’s bier still stood in the main hall she had Tian Sheng executed.

sentences.zh: 又欲盡誅諸貴人，中常侍管霸、蘇康苦諫，乃止。

translations.literal: Again she wished to kill all the noble ladies; Regular Palace Attendants Guan Ba and Su Kang bitterly remonstrated; then she stopped.
translations.idiomatic: She would have slaughtered every noble lady the late emperor had favored had not eunuchs Guan Ba and Su Kang pleaded until she relented.

sentences.zh: 時太后父大將軍武謀誅宦官，而中常侍曹節等矯詔殺武，遷太后於南宮雲臺，家屬徙比景。

translations.literal: At the time the empress dowager’s father Grand General Wu plotted to execute the palace attendants, but Regular Palace Attendants Cao Jie and others forged an edict and killed Wu, moved the empress dowager to South Palace Cloud Terrace, and families were moved to Bijin.
translations.idiomatic: When her father Dou Wu moved against the eunuchs, Cao Jie and his faction forged an edict, killed Dou Wu, confined the empress dowager to the Cloud Terrace in the southern palace, and banished her kin to Bijin.

sentences.zh: 孝仁董皇后

translations.literal: Empress Dong the Xiaoren.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Dong, mother of Emperor Ling (posthumously titled Xiaoren, “filial and benevolent”)

sentences.zh: 孝仁董皇后諱某，河間人。

translations.literal: Empress Dong the Xiaoren had taboo name so-and-so; she was a native of Hejian.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Dong’s personal name was not recorded in the histories; she came from Hejian.

sentences.zh: 為解犢亭侯萇夫人，生靈帝。

translations.literal: She was wife to Marquis of Jieduting Chang and bore Emperor Ling.
translations.idiomatic: She was the wife of Liu Chang, marquis of Jieduting, and bore Liu Hong, who became Emperor Ling.

sentences.zh: 建寧元年，帝即位，追尊萇為孝仁皇，陵曰慎陵，以后為慎園貴人。

translations.literal: In the first year of Jianning, when the emperor took the throne, they posthumously honored Chang as Emperor Xiaoren, the tomb called Shen Tomb, and made the empress Noble Lady of Shen Park.
translations.idiomatic: When her son mounted the throne in 168, Liu Chang was posthumously titled Emperor Xiaoren with a mausoleum named Shen; she herself was given the title noble lady of the Shen park estate.

sentences.zh: 及竇氏誅，明年，帝使中常侍迎貴人，並徵貴人兄寵到京師，上尊號曰孝仁皇后，居南宮嘉德殿，宮稱永樂。

translations.literal: When the Dou clan was executed, the next year the emperor sent a Regular Palace Attendant to welcome the noble lady and also summoned the noble lady’s elder brother Chong to the capital, elevated her honorable title as Empress Xiaoren, dwelt at South Palace Jiade Hall, the palace being called Yongle.
translations.idiomatic: After the Dou purge in 169 the emperor summoned her to the capital along with her brother Dong Chong, invested her as Empress Xiaoren, and installed her in the Jiade Hall of the southern palace in a compound known as Yongle.

sentences.zh: 拜寵執金吾。

translations.literal: Chong was appointed Bearer of the Mace.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Chong was appointed bearer of the golden mace.

sentences.zh: 後坐矯稱永樂后屬請，下獄死。

translations.literal: Later, sitting guilt for falsely claiming Yongle empress’s kin and requesting favors, he was cast into prison and died.
translations.idiomatic: Later he was jailed and died for falsely claiming connections to the Yongle empress’s household to solicit favors.

sentences.zh: 及竇太后崩，始與朝政，使帝賣官求貨，自納金錢，盈滿堂室。

translations.literal: When Empress Dowager Dou died, she first joined court government, causing the emperor to sell offices seeking goods, herself receiving gold and cash, filling halls and chambers.
translations.idiomatic: After Empress Dowager Dou’s death she began to meddle in government, pushed Emperor Ling to sell offices for cash, and pocketed so much gold and silver that her rooms overflowed.

sentences.zh: 中平五年，以后兄子衛尉脩侯重為票騎將軍，領兵千餘人。

translations.literal: In the fifth year of Zhongping, they made the empress’s elder brother’s son, Commandant of the Guards Marquis of Xiu Chong, General of Agile Cavalry, leading more than a thousand troops.
translations.idiomatic: In 188 her nephew Dong Chong, marquis of Xiu and commandant of the guards, was named general of agile cavalry with a personal guard of over a thousand.

sentences.zh: 初，后自養皇子協，數勸帝立為太子，而何皇后恨之，議未及定而帝崩。

translations.literal: At first the empress herself nurtured the imperial son Xie, repeatedly urging the emperor to establish him as heir apparent, but Empress He hated it; deliberation had not reached settlement when the emperor died.
translations.idiomatic: She had raised Prince Xie herself and repeatedly urged Emperor Ling to make him crown prince; Empress He bitterly opposed the idea, and before it could be settled the emperor died.

sentences.zh: 何太后臨朝，重與太后兄大將軍進權勢相害，后每欲參幹政事，太后輒相禁塞。

translations.literal: Empress Dowager He faced the court; Chong and the empress dowager’s elder brother Grand General Jin’s power positions mutually harmed each other; whenever the empress wished to join and interfere in government affairs, the empress dowager then mutually blocked and stopped her.
translations.idiomatic: Under Empress Dowager He’s regency Dong Chong clashed with her brother He Jin; whenever Empress Dong tried to intervene in state affairs, the empress dowager blocked her.

sentences.zh: 后忿恚詈言曰：『汝今輈張，怙汝兄耶？

translations.literal: The empress in anger and resentment cursed, saying, ‘You now are zhou-zhang, relying on your elder brother?
translations.idiomatic: Furious, Empress Dong shouted that she strutted about only because her brother backed her.

sentences.zh: 當勑票騎斷何進頭來。

translations.literal: I shall order the Agile Cavalry to cut off He Jin’s head and bring it.
translations.idiomatic: She would have her general of agile cavalry bring her He Jin’s head.

sentences.zh: 』何太后聞，以告進。

translations.literal: ’ Empress Dowager He heard and reported to Jin.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Dowager He heard and told He Jin.

sentences.zh: 進與三公及弟車騎將軍苗等奏：『孝仁皇后使故中常侍夏惲、永樂太僕封諝等交通州郡，辜較在所珍寶貨賂，悉入西省。

translations.literal: Jin together with the Three Excellencies and his younger brother General of Chariots and Cavalry Miao and others memorialized, saying, ‘Empress Xiaoren caused former Regular Palace Attendant Xia Yun, Yongle Grand Coachman Feng Xu, and others to traffic with prefectures and commanderies, forcibly taking precious treasures and bribes where they were, all entering the Western Bureau.
translations.idiomatic: He Jin and the Three Excellencies, with his brother He Miao the general of chariots and cavalry, memorialized that Empress Dong had used the former eunuch Xia Yun, the Yongle grand coachman Feng Xu, and others to collude with local officials, extort treasure and bribes, and funnel everything into her private treasury at the Western Bureau.

sentences.zh: 蕃后故事不得留京師，輿服有章，膳羞有品。

translations.literal: By the precedent for consorts of vassal kings (藩后), she may not remain in the capital; carriage and dress have regulations; viands have grades.
translations.idiomatic: Statute held that a king’s mother could not reside in the capital; her carriages, robes, and fare had to observe fixed rank.

sentences.zh: 請永樂后遷宮本國。

translations.literal: We request the Yongle empress remove her palace to her native state.
translations.idiomatic: They asked that the Yongle empress be sent back to her princely fief in Hejian.

sentences.zh: 』奏可。

translations.literal: ’ The memorial was approved.
translations.idiomatic: The throne approved.

sentences.zh: 何進遂舉兵圍驃騎府，收重，重免官自殺。

translations.literal: He Jin thereupon raised troops and surrounded the Agile Cavalry’s residence, seized Chong; Chong was stripped of office and committed suicide.
translations.idiomatic: He Jin then surrounded Dong Chong’s mansion with troops, arrested him, and Chong killed himself after being dismissed.

sentences.zh: 后憂怖，疾病暴崩，在位二十二年。

translations.literal: The empress was anxious and frightened; illness; sudden death; having held position twenty-two years.
translations.idiomatic: Stricken with fear and illness, she died suddenly after twenty-two years as empress dowager.

sentences.zh: 民間歸咎何氏。

translations.literal: Among the people blame was returned to the He clan.
translations.idiomatic: Popular opinion blamed the He family.

sentences.zh: 喪還河間，合葬慎陵。

translations.literal: The bier returned to Hejian; joint burial at Shen Tomb.
translations.idiomatic: Her remains were returned to Hejian for burial with Liu Chang at the Shen mausoleum.

sentences.zh: 靈帝宋皇后

translations.literal: Empress Song of Emperor Ling.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Song of Emperor Ling

sentences.zh: 靈帝宋皇后諱某，扶風平陵人也，肅宗宋貴人之從曾孫也。

translations.literal: Empress Song of Emperor Ling had taboo name so-and-so; she was a native of Pingling in Fufeng, a great-grandniece on the elder-brother line of Empress Suzong’s Noble Lady Song.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Song’s given name was not preserved; she came from Pingling in Fufeng and was a collateral descendant of Emperor Zhang’s Lady Song.

sentences.zh: 建寧三年，選入掖庭為貴人。

translations.literal: In the third year of Jianning, by selection she entered the Rear Palace as a noble lady.
translations.idiomatic: In 170 she was chosen for the harem as a noble lady.

sentences.zh: 明年，立為皇后。

translations.literal: The next year, she was established as empress.
translations.idiomatic: The following year she became empress.

sentences.zh: 父酆，執金吾，封不其鄉侯。

translations.literal: Her father Feng was Bearer of the Mace, enfeoffed as Marquis of Buqi village.
translations.idiomatic: Her father Song Feng served as bearer of the golden mace and held the village marquisate of Buqi.

sentences.zh: 帝後夢見桓帝怒曰：『宋皇后有何罪過，而聽用邪孽，使絕其命？

translations.literal: Later the emperor dreamed he saw Emperor Huan angrily say, ‘What crime or fault had Empress Song, that you listened to and used evil spawn, causing her life to be cut off?
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Ling later dreamed that Emperor Huan rebuked him for destroying Empress Song without cause, having listened to villains.

sentences.zh: 勃海王悝既已自貶，又受誅斃。

translations.literal: Prince Hui of Bohai had already demoted himself, again received execution and death.
translations.idiomatic: Prince Liu Kui of Bohai had already humbled himself in submission, yet you still had him killed.

sentences.zh: 今宋氏及悝自訴於天，上帝震怒，罪在難救。

translations.literal: Now the Song clan and Kui have themselves appealed to Heaven; High God is greatly angry; the guilt is hard to save.
translations.idiomatic: Now the Song family and Liu Kui cry their wrongs to Heaven; the Lord on High is enraged, and your guilt may be beyond remedy.

sentences.zh: 』夢殊明察。

translations.literal: ’ The dream was extremely clear and bright.
translations.idiomatic: The dream was vivid as waking sight.

sentences.zh: 帝既覺而恐，以事問於羽林左監許永曰：『此何祥？

translations.literal: When the emperor awoke he was frightened; he asked the matter of Left Supervisor of the Feathered Forest Xu Yong, saying, ‘What omen is this?
translations.idiomatic: He woke in terror and asked Xu Yong, left supervisor of the household guard, what the omen portended.

sentences.zh: 其可攘乎？

translations.literal: Can it be averted?
translations.idiomatic: Could it be turned aside?

sentences.zh: 』永對曰：『宋皇后親與陛下共承宗廟，母臨萬國，歷年已久，海內蒙化，過惡無聞。

translations.literal: ’ Yong replied, saying, ‘Empress Song personally with Your Majesty jointly received the ancestral temple; as mother she faced the myriad states; the years passed have already been long; within the seas they received transformation; of faults and evil none was heard.
translations.idiomatic: Xu Yong answered that Empress Song had shared the charge of the ancestral shrines and stood as mother of the realm for many years, and the empire had heard no fault in her.

sentences.zh: 而虛聽讒妒之說，以致無辜之罪，身嬰極誅，禍及家族，天下臣妾，咸為怨痛。

translations.literal: Yet you emptily listened to slanderous and jealous talk, thereby bringing innocent guilt; her person bore extreme execution; disaster reached clan and family; all the empire’s ministers and concubines alike were resentful and pained.
translations.idiomatic: Yet you believed slander out of jealousy, condemned an innocent woman to death, ruined her whole clan, and left every subject in the realm grieving and angry.

sentences.zh: 勃海王悝，桓帝母弟也。

translations.literal: Prince Hui of Bohai, Kui, was Emperor Huan’s younger brother by the same mother.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Kui, prince of Bohai, was Emperor Huan’s full brother.

sentences.zh: 處國奉藩，未嘗有過。

translations.literal: Dwelling in his state, serving as vassal, he never had fault.
translations.idiomatic: He held his fief as a vassal king and gave no cause for offense.

sentences.zh: 陛下曾不證審，遂伏其辜。

translations.literal: Your Majesty never verified and examined; thereupon he submitted to his guilt.
translations.idiomatic: You never looked into the facts before you destroyed him.

sentences.zh: 昔晉侯失刑，亦夢大厲被發屬地。

translations.literal: Formerly when the Marquis of Jin lost in punishments, he also dreamed a great malice with hair loose touching the ground.
translations.idiomatic: When the marquis of Jin misused punishment in antiquity, he dreamed of a vengeful ghost with disheveled hair dragging on the ground.

sentences.zh: 天道明察，鬼神難誣。

translations.literal: Heaven’s Way is clear and bright; ghosts and spirits are hard to deceive.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven sees clearly; the dead are not easily mocked.

sentences.zh: 宜並改葬，以安冤魂。

translations.literal: It is fitting jointly to re-bury, thereby to pacify wronged souls.
translations.idiomatic: Give them honorable reburial and so quiet their restless spirits.

sentences.zh: 反宋后之徙家，復勃海之先封，以消厥咎。

translations.literal: Return the Song empress’s moved household; restore Bohai’s former enfeoffment; thereby to dispel that fault.
translations.idiomatic: Recall the Song kin from exile, restore Liu Kui’s heirs to their old title, and thus answer Heaven’s reproach.

sentences.zh: 』帝弗能用，尋亦崩焉。

translations.literal: ’ The emperor was not able to use it; soon he also died.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Ling did not act on the advice; before long he too was dead.

sentences.zh: 靈思何皇后

translations.literal: Empress He the Lingsi.
translations.idiomatic: Empress He of Emperor Ling (posthumously titled Lingsi)

sentences.zh: 靈思何皇后諱某，南陽宛人。

translations.literal: Empress He the Lingsi had taboo name so-and-so; she was a native of Wan in Nanyang.
translations.idiomatic: Empress He’s given name was not recorded; she came from Wan in Nanyang commandery.

sentences.zh: 家本屠者，以選入掖庭。

translations.literal: Her family was originally butchers; by selection she entered the Rear Palace.
translations.idiomatic: Her family were butchers by trade; she was chosen for the harem.

sentences.zh: 長七尺一寸。

translations.literal: Her height was seven chi one cun.
translations.idiomatic: She stood seven feet one inch tall by Han measure.

sentences.zh: 生皇子辯，養於史道人家，號曰史侯。

translations.literal: She bore the imperial son Bian, who was reared in Historian Daoist’s household, called the Marquis of Shi.
translations.idiomatic: She bore Prince Bian, who was fostered in the household of a Taoist surnamed Shi and nicknamed the Marquis of Shi.

sentences.zh: 拜后為貴人，甚有寵幸。

translations.literal: She was appointed noble lady, greatly having favor and love.
translations.idiomatic: She was raised to noble lady and became a great favorite.

sentences.zh: 性彊忌，後宮莫不震懾。

translations.literal: By nature strong and jealous, none in the rear palace did not tremble in awe.
translations.idiomatic: Fierce and jealous by nature, she terrified every woman in the harem.

sentences.zh: 光和三年，立為皇后。

translations.literal: In the third year of Guanghe, she was established as empress.
translations.idiomatic: In 180 she was made empress.

sentences.zh: 明年，追號后父真為車騎將軍、舞陽宣德侯，因封后母興為舞陽君。

translations.literal: The next year, they posthumously titled the empress’s father Zhen General of Chariots and Cavalry Marquis Xuande of Wuyang, and thereupon enfeoffed the empress’s mother Xing as Lady of Wuyang.
translations.idiomatic: The next year her late father He Zhen was posthumously titled general of chariots and cavalry and marquis Xuande of Wuyang, and her mother Xing was created lady of Wuyang.

sentences.zh: 時王美人任娠，畏后，乃服藥欲除之，而胎安不動，又數夢負日而行。

translations.literal: At the time the Beauty Wang was pregnant; fearing the empress, she then took drugs wishing to remove it, but the fetus settled and did not move; again she repeatedly dreamed of carrying the sun on her back and walking.
translations.idiomatic: Lady Wang the Beauty, pregnant and afraid of the empress, took drugs to abort the child, but the pregnancy held; she dreamed more than once that she was walking with the sun on her back.

sentences.zh: 四年，生皇子協，后遂鴆殺美人。

translations.literal: In the fourth year, she bore the imperial son Xie; the empress then poisoned the beauty to death.
translations.idiomatic: In 181 she bore Prince Xie; the empress then poisoned Lady Wang to death.

sentences.zh: 帝大怒，欲廢后，諸宦官固請得止。

translations.literal: The emperor was greatly angry and wished to depose the empress; the various palace attendants firmly requested and obtained stopping.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Ling was furious and meant to depose her, but the eunuchs begged him until he relented.

sentences.zh: 董太后自養協，號曰董侯。

translations.literal: Empress Dowager Dong herself reared Xie, called the Marquis of Dong.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Dowager Dong took the boy in and he was known as the Marquis of Dong.

sentences.zh: 王美人，趙國人也。

translations.literal: The Beauty Wang was a native of Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: Lady Wang the Beauty came from the princedom of Zhao.

sentences.zh: 祖父苞，五官中郎將。

translations.literal: Her grandfather Bao was Gentleman of the Household for All Purposes.
translations.idiomatic: Her grandfather Wang Bao had been a gentleman of the household for all purposes at court.

sentences.zh: 美人豐姿色，聰敏有才明，能書會計，以良家子應法相選入掖庭。

translations.literal: The beauty was abundant in posture and color, clever and keen with talent and brightness, able to write and reckon accounts; as a good-family daughter she answered to physiognomy selection and entered the Rear Palace.
translations.idiomatic: Lady Wang was lovely and quick-witted, literate and good with figures; as a girl from a respectable house she passed the court’s face-reading selection for the harem.

sentences.zh: 帝愍協早失母，又思美人，作《追德賦》、《令儀頌》。

translations.literal: The emperor pitied Xie for early losing his mother, and again missed the beauty, composing the Rhapsody on Pursuing Virtue and Ode to Exemplary Bearing.
translations.idiomatic: Grieving that Liu Xie had lost his mother so young and longing for Lady Wang, Emperor Ling wrote the Rhapsody on Pursuing Virtue and the Ode to Exemplary Bearing.

sentences.zh: 中平六年，帝崩，皇子辯即位，尊后為皇太后。

translations.literal: In the sixth year of Zhongping, the emperor died; the imperial son Bian took the throne; they honored the empress as empress dowager.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Ling died in 189, Liu Bian succeeded and his mother became empress dowager.

sentences.zh: 太后臨朝。

translations.literal: The empress dowager faced the court.
translations.idiomatic: She assumed the regency.

sentences.zh: 后兄大將軍進欲誅宦官，反為所害；

translations.literal: The empress’s elder brother Grand General Jin wished to execute the palace attendants, but on the contrary was harmed by them;
translations.idiomatic: Her brother He Jin tried to destroy the eunuchs and was killed by them instead;

sentences.zh: 舞陽君亦為亂兵所殺。

translations.literal: the Lady of Wuyang was also killed by mutinous troops.
translations.idiomatic: and Lady He of Wuyang, the empress dowager’s mother, fell to the same mutiny.

sentences.zh: 幷州牧董卓被徵，將兵入洛陽，陵虐朝廷，遂廢少帝為弘農王而立協，是為獻帝。

translations.literal: Governor of Bingzhou Dong Zhuo was summoned, led troops into Luoyang, violated and abused the court, thereupon deposed the young emperor as Prince of Hongnong and established Xie, who is Emperor Xian.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhuo, governor of Bing, marched on Luoyang, cowed the court, deposed Emperor Shao as prince of Hongnong, and set Liu Xie on the throne as Emperor Xian.

sentences.zh: 扶弘農王下殿，北面稱臣。

translations.literal: They helped the Prince of Hongnong down from the hall, facing north to declare themselves subjects.
translations.idiomatic: The deposed emperor was led from the dais to kowtow as a subject facing north.

sentences.zh: 太后鯁涕，群臣含悲，莫敢言。

translations.literal: The empress dowager choked with tears; the host of ministers held grief in their mouths; none dared speak.
translations.idiomatic: The empress dowager wept silently; the ministers grieved but none dared speak.

sentences.zh: 董卓又議太后踧迫永樂宮，至令憂死，逆婦姑之禮，乃遷於永安宮，因進酖，弒而崩。

translations.literal: Dong Zhuo again debated that the empress dowager had cramped and pressed the Yongle palace, reaching to cause worry-death, contrary to the rites of daughter-in-law and mother-in-law; then moved her to Yong’an Palace, thereupon advancing poisoned wine, assassinated, and she died.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhuo accused her of hounding Empress Dowager Dong at Yongle to her death—a breach of duty between daughter- and mother-in-law—had her moved to Yong’an Palace, and sent poisoned wine; she was murdered.

sentences.zh: 在位十年。

translations.literal: Having held position ten years.
translations.idiomatic: She had held power for ten years.

sentences.zh: 董卓令帝出奉常亭舉哀，公卿皆白衣會，不成喪也。

translations.literal: Dong Zhuo ordered the emperor to go out to Fengchangting to raise mourning; dukes and ministers all in white gathered; it did not complete mourning.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhuo made Emperor Xian hold a sham wake for her at Fengchang Pavilion, with the high ministers in white—it was no proper funeral.

sentences.zh: 合葬文昭陵。

translations.literal: Joint burial at Wenzhao Tomb.
translations.idiomatic: She was buried with Emperor Ling at the Wenzhao mausoleum.

sentences.zh: 初，太后新立，當竭二祖廟，欲齋，輒有變故，如此者數，竟不克。

translations.literal: At first, when the empress dowager was newly established, she ought to have exhausted service at the two Founders’ temples; wishing to fast, then there were incidents; thus several times; ultimately she did not succeed.
translations.idiomatic: When she first became empress dowager she was to perform the great sacrifice at the shrines of the two founder emperors; each time she prepared to fast, some mishap intervened, and after several attempts the rite was never completed.

sentences.zh: 時有識之士心獨怪之，後遂因何氏傾沒漢祚焉。

translations.literal: At the time men of insight in their hearts alone marveled at it; afterward then by the He clan the Han mandate was overturned and lost.
translations.idiomatic: Thoughtful observers took it for an ill omen—and in the end the He clan did bring down the house of Han.

sentences.zh: 明年，山東義兵大起，討董卓之亂。

translations.literal: The next year, righteous troops east of the mountains rose greatly, punishing Dong Zhuo’s rebellion.
translations.idiomatic: The next year the eastern lords raised armies against Dong Zhuo.

sentences.zh: 卓乃置弘農王於閣上，使郎中令李儒進酖，曰：『服此藥，可以辟惡。

translations.literal: Zhuo then placed the Prince of Hongnong on a tower, ordered the palace superintendent Li Ru to advance poisoned wine, saying, ‘Drink this medicine; it can ward off evil.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhuo had the prince taken to a pavilion and sent Li Ru with poisoned wine, claiming it was medicine to ward off ill vapors.

sentences.zh: 』王曰：『我無疾，是欲殺我耳！

translations.literal: ’ The prince said, ‘I have no illness; this is wishing to kill me!
translations.idiomatic: The prince replied that he was not sick and that they meant to murder him.

sentences.zh: 』不肯飲。

translations.literal: ’ He refused to drink.
translations.idiomatic: He would not drink.

sentences.zh: 強飲之，不得已，乃與妻唐姬及宮人飲宴別。

translations.literal: They forced him to drink it; having no choice, then with his wife Lady Tang and palace women he drank a farewell banquet.
translations.idiomatic: They forced the cup on him; he gave way and held a last feast with his consort Lady Tang and her attendants.

sentences.zh: 酒行，王悲歌曰：『天道易兮我何艱！

translations.literal: As the wine circulated, the prince sang sadly, saying, ‘Heaven’s way shifts—how hard my lot!
translations.idiomatic: Mid-cup he sang: Heaven turns easy while I am crushed with hardship!

sentences.zh: 棄萬乘兮退守蕃。

translations.literal: Cast off the ten thousand chariots—withdraw to guard a border fief.
translations.idiomatic: Stripped of the throne, I am cast back to a prince’s narrow domain!

sentences.zh: 逆臣見迫兮命不延，逝將去汝兮適幽玄！

translations.literal: Rebellious ministers press me—my life will not extend; I am about to leave you—going to dim darkness!
translations.idiomatic: Rebel ministers hound me—my days are cut short; I leave you now for the dark world below!

sentences.zh: 』因令唐姬起舞，姬抗袖而歌曰：『皇天崩兮后土穨，身為帝兮命夭摧。

translations.literal: ’ Thereupon he ordered Lady Tang to rise in dance; she raised her sleeves and sang, saying, ‘August Heaven collapses—hou-earth crumbles; body being emperor—fate is cut off young.
translations.idiomatic: He bade Lady Tang dance; lifting her sleeves she sang: The sky has fallen, the earth gives way; I was wife to a Son of Heaven—yet fate tears us apart in youth!

sentences.zh: 死生路異兮從此乖，奈我煢獨兮心中哀！

translations.literal: Death and life, roads different—from here we part; what of my solitary alone—grief in the heart!
translations.idiomatic: Living and dead walk separate roads—we part here; I am left alone, and grief fills my breast!

sentences.zh: 』因泣下嗚咽，坐者皆歔欷。

translations.literal: ’ Thereupon tears fell choked; those seated all sniffled.
translations.idiomatic: She wept aloud; every guest wept with her.

sentences.zh: 王謂姬曰：『卿王者妃，勢不復為吏民妻。

translations.literal: The prince said to the lady, ‘You are a king’s consort; in the situation you will not again become wife to clerk or commoner.
translations.idiomatic: He told her she had been a prince’s consort and could never again be a commoner’s wife.

sentences.zh: 自愛，從此長辭！

translations.literal: Love yourself—from here long farewell!
translations.idiomatic: He urged her to guard herself well, for this was their last farewell.

sentences.zh: 』遂飲藥而死。

translations.literal: ’ Thereupon he drank the drug and died.
translations.idiomatic: He drained the poison and died.

sentences.zh: 時年十八。

translations.literal: At the time his age was eighteen.
translations.idiomatic: He was eighteen years old.

sentences.zh: 唐姬，潁川人也。

translations.literal: Lady Tang was a native of Yingchuan.
translations.idiomatic: Lady Tang came from Yingchuan.

sentences.zh: 王薨，歸鄉里。

translations.literal: When the prince died, she returned to her village.
translations.idiomatic: After the prince’s death she went home to her family.

sentences.zh: 父會稽太守瑁欲嫁之，姬誓不許。

translations.literal: Her father Mao, Administrator of Kuaiji, wished to marry her off; the lady swore and would not permit.
translations.idiomatic: Her father Tang Mao, governor of Kuaiji, tried to remarry her; she swore she would not.

sentences.zh: 及李傕破長安，遣兵鈔關東，略得姬。

translations.literal: When Li Jue broke Chang’an, he sent troops to raid east of the passes and seized the lady.
translations.idiomatic: When Li Jue sacked Chang’an he sent raiders east of the passes who carried her off.

sentences.zh: 傕因欲妻之，固不聽，而終不自名。

translations.literal: Jue thereupon wished to take her as wife; she firmly would not listen, yet to the end did not declare her own name.
translations.idiomatic: Li Jue meant to make her his concubine; she refused steadfastly and never revealed who she was.

sentences.zh: 尚書賈詡知之，以狀白獻帝。

translations.literal: Minister Jia Xu learned of it and reported the circumstances to Emperor Xian.
translations.idiomatic: Minister Jia Xu learned her story and told Emperor Xian.

sentences.zh: 帝聞感愴，乃下詔迎姬，置園中，使侍中持節拜為弘農王妃。

translations.literal: When the emperor heard, he was moved and grieved; then he issued an edict welcoming the lady, placing her in a park, and ordered a Palace Attendant bearing staff of authority to invest her as consort to the Prince of Hongnong.
translations.idiomatic: Moved, Emperor Xian summoned her to the capital, housed her in a palace garden, and sent a palace attendant with the imperial staff to invest her formally as the late prince’s consort.

sentences.zh: 初平元年二月，葬弘農王於故中常侍趙忠成壙中，謚曰懷王。

translations.literal: In the second month of the first year of Chuping, they buried the Prince of Hongnong in the completed tomb pit of former Regular Palace Attendant Zhao Zhong, posthumous title Prince Huai.
translations.idiomatic: In February 190 he was buried in the tomb vault prepared for the eunuch Zhao Zhong and posthumously titled Prince Huai, “the pitied.”

sentences.zh: 帝求母王美人兄斌，斌將妻子詣長安，賜第宅田業，拜奉車都尉。

translations.literal: The emperor sought his mother the Beauty Wang’s elder brother Bin; Bin led wife and children to Chang’an; he was gifted residence, fields, and estates; appointed Colonel Director of Retinue.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Xian sought out Lady Wang’s brother Wang Bin, who came to Chang’an with his family; the emperor gave him a mansion, land, and the title colonel director of the imperial carriages.

sentences.zh: 興平元年，帝加元服。

translations.literal: In the first year of Xingping, the emperor added the capping.
translations.idiomatic: In 194 Emperor Xian underwent the capping ceremony and came of age.

sentences.zh: 有司奏立長秋宮。

translations.literal: The responsible offices memorialized to establish the Palace of Long Autumn.
translations.idiomatic: Officials petitioned to install an empress.

sentences.zh: 詔曰：『朕稟受不弘，遭值禍亂，未能紹先，以光故典。

translations.literal: An edict said, ‘We have received endowment not great, encountered disaster and chaos, been unable to continue the former, to brighten old canons.
translations.idiomatic: His edict read that his own virtue was slight, that he had lived through rebellion, and that he had failed to carry forward his forebears’ glory.

sentences.zh: 皇母前薨，未卜宅兆，禮章有闕，中心如結。

translations.literal: Imperial Mother before died; we have not divined tomb site; ritual regulations have gaps; the center of the heart is as knotted.
translations.idiomatic: His imperial mother had died before her tomb could be sited; the rites remained incomplete, and his heart was bound in grief.

sentences.zh: 三歲之慼，蓋不言吉，且須其後。

translations.literal: The three years’ sorrow—generally one does not speak of auspiciousness; for the moment we must wait for afterward.
translations.idiomatic: The three years’ mourning for a parent forbade auspicious undertakings, so naming an empress had to wait.

sentences.zh: 』於是有司乃奏追尊王美人為靈懷皇后，改葬文昭陵，儀比敬、恭二陵，使光祿大夫持節行司空事奉璽綬，斌與河南尹駱業復土。

translations.literal: ’ Thereupon the responsible offices memorialized to posthumously honor the Beauty Wang as Empress Linghuai, re-bury at Wenzhao Tomb, ceremony compared to the two tombs Jing and Gong, sent Grand Counsellor bearing staff acting as Minister of Works to present seals and ribbons, Bin together with Administrator of Henan Luo Ye completed the earth.
translations.idiomatic: Officials then memorialized to posthumously title Lady Wang Empress Linghuai, reinter her at the Wenzhao mausoleum with rites matching the Jing and Gong imperial tombs; a grand counsellor acting as minister of works carried the seals and ribbons, while Wang Bin and Henan governor Luo Ye oversaw the reburial.

sentences.zh: 斌還，遷執金吾，封都亭侯，食邑五百戶。

translations.literal: When Bin returned, he was moved to Bearer of the Mace, enfeoffed as Marquis of Duting, revenue five hundred households.
translations.idiomatic: On his return Wang Bin was promoted bearer of the golden mace and created marquis of Duting with five hundred households.

sentences.zh: 病卒，贈前將軍印綬，謁者監護喪事。
