translations.literal: He died of illness; posthumous gift General of the Van seals and ribbons; heralds supervised the funeral.
translations.idiomatic: He died in office and was posthumously awarded general of the van; court heralds supervised his obsequies.

sentences.zh: 長子端襲爵。

translations.literal: The eldest son Duan succeeded to the enfeoffment.
translations.idiomatic: His eldest son Wang Duan inherited the title.

sentences.zh: 獻帝伏皇后

translations.literal: Empress Fu of Emperor Xian.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Fu of Emperor Xian

sentences.zh: 獻帝伏皇后諱壽，瑯邪東武人，大司徒湛之八世孫也。

translations.literal: Empress Fu of Emperor Xian had the taboo name Shou; she was a native of Dongwu in Langye, eighth-generation descendant of Grand Minister over the Masses Zhan.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Fu Shou came from Dongwu in Langye and was the eighth-generation descendant of the Eastern Han grand minister Fu Zhan.

sentences.zh: 父完，沈深有大度，襲爵不其侯，尚桓帝女陽安公主，為侍中。

translations.literal: Her father Wan was deep and still with great capacity; he inherited the Marquis of Buqi enfeoffment, married Emperor Huan’s daughter the Princess of Yang’an, and was Palace Attendant.
translations.idiomatic: Her father Fu Wan, a man of depth and judgment, inherited the Buqi marquisate, married Emperor Huan’s daughter the princess of Yang’an, and served as palace attendant.

sentences.zh: 自帝都許，守位而已，宿衛兵侍，莫非曹氏黨舊姻戚。

translations.literal: Since the emperor established the capital at Xu, he keeps position only; night-guard soldiers and attendants—none are not of the Cao clan faction, old allies, or marriage kin.
translations.idiomatic: After the court moved to Xu the Son of Heaven held a hollow title: every man at arms in the palace was a Cao partisan or kinsman by marriage.

sentences.zh: 議郎趙彥嘗為帝陳言時策，曹操惡而殺之。

translations.literal: Consultant Zhao Yan once for the emperor set forth words on timely policy; Cao Cao hated him and killed him.
translations.idiomatic: Consultant Zhao Yan had urged timely policies on the emperor; Cao Cao resented it and had him executed.

sentences.zh: 其餘內外，多見誅戮。

translations.literal: The rest within and without mostly saw execution and slaughter.
translations.idiomatic: Scores of others at court and in the provinces shared the same fate.

sentences.zh: 操後以事入見殿中，帝不任其憤，因曰：『君若能相輔，則厚；

translations.literal: Afterward Cao Cao on business entered to be seen in the hall; the emperor could not bear his resentment, therefore saying, ‘If you are able to assist Us, then it is generous;
translations.idiomatic: Once when Cao Cao came to audience the emperor could not hold back his anger and said that if Cao would truly support the throne, the emperor would be deeply grateful;

sentences.zh: 不爾，幸垂恩相舍。

translations.literal: If not so, We hope you will graciously grant favor and release Us.
translations.idiomatic: if not, he begged Cao in mercy to let him go.

sentences.zh: 』操失色，俯仰求出。

translations.literal: ’ Cao Cao lost color, looking up and down seeking to leave.
translations.idiomatic: Cao Cao turned pale and begged leave to withdraw.

sentences.zh: 舊儀，三公領兵朝見，令虎賁執刃挾之。

translations.literal: By old ritual, when the Three Excellencies led troops to court audience, they ordered Rapid-as-Tigers to hold blades and flank them.
translations.idiomatic: By old rule, high ministers who commanded troops entered audience flanked by guards with drawn blades.

sentences.zh: 操出，顧左右，汗流浹背，自後不敢復朝請。

translations.literal: When Cao Cao went out, he looked left and right, sweat flowing soaking his back; from then he did not dare again to court audience on summons.
translations.idiomatic: He left drenched in sweat, glancing at the guards, and never again attended a personal audience with the emperor.

sentences.zh: 董承女為貴人，操誅承而求貴人殺之。

translations.literal: Dong Cheng’s daughter was a noble lady; Cao Cao executed Cheng and sought the noble lady to kill her.
translations.idiomatic: When Cao Cao executed Dong Cheng he also demanded the life of Dong’s daughter, who was a consort of the emperor.

sentences.zh: 帝以貴人有妊，累為請，不能得。

translations.literal: The emperor because the noble lady had pregnancy, repeatedly pleaded for her, could not obtain.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor pleaded repeatedly because she was pregnant, but Cao Cao would not relent.

sentences.zh: 後自是懷懼，乃與父完書，言曹操殘逼之狀，令密圖之。

translations.literal: The empress from this harbored fear; then she wrote her father Wan a letter, stating Cao Cao’s cruel oppression’s condition, ordering secretly to plot against him.
translations.idiomatic: The empress grew afraid and wrote secretly to Fu Wan describing Cao Cao’s cruelty and urging him to plot against him in secret.

sentences.zh: 完不敢發，至十九年，事乃露泄。

translations.literal: Wan did not dare initiate; reaching the nineteenth year, the affair then leaked.
translations.idiomatic: Fu Wan did not dare act; in the nineteenth year of Jian’an the plot leaked out.

sentences.zh: 操追大怒，遂逼帝廢後，假為策曰：『皇後壽，得由卑賤，登顯尊極，自處椒房，二紀於茲。

translations.literal: Cao Cao thereupon was greatly angry, then compelled the emperor to depose the empress, falsely making a rescript saying, ‘Empress Shou, obtaining from low and base, ascended to manifest utmost honor, dwelling in the pepper chamber—two twelve-year cycles here.
translations.idiomatic: Cao Cao, enraged, forced Emperor Xian to depose her and forged an edict: Fu Shou had risen from humble rank to empress and had occupied the inner palace for twenty-four years.

sentences.zh: 既無任、姒徽音之美，又乏謹身養己之福，而陰懷妒害，苞藏禍心，弗可以承天命，奉祖宗。

translations.literal: She has neither the fine sound of Ren and Si, nor the fortune of careful body and nurturing self; yet secretly harbors jealousy and harm, wrapping and concealing a baleful heart—she cannot receive Heaven’s mandate or serve ancestors.
translations.idiomatic: It charged that she lacked the virtue of ancient consorts Ren and Si, showed no modest care for herself, nursed jealousy, and hid treasonous intent—unfit to receive Heaven’s mandate or honor the imperial ancestors.

sentences.zh: 今使禦史大夫郗慮持節策詔，其上皇後璽綬，退避中宮，遷於它館。

translations.literal: Now We send Imperial Counsellor Xi Lu bearing staff with rescript edict: hand up the empress’s seals and ribbons, withdraw from the central palace, move to another lodge.
translations.idiomatic: Imperial Counsellor Xi Lu was ordered to take the staff of authority, receive her seals and ribbons, remove her from the empress’s quarters, and confine her to another residence.

sentences.zh: 鳴呼傷哉！

translations.literal: Alas, how wounding!
translations.idiomatic: Alas, what a bitter decree!

sentences.zh: 自壽取之，未致於理，為幸多焉。

translations.literal: Shou brought it on herself; We have not brought her to the principle—there is much fortune.
translations.idiomatic: The forgery claimed she had brought this on herself and was spared a worse fate than she deserved.

sentences.zh: 』又以尚書令華歆為郗慮副，勒兵入宮收後。

translations.literal: ’ Again he made Minister of the Secretariat Hua Xin Xi Lu’s deputy, pressing troops into the palace to seize the empress.
translations.idiomatic: Minister Hua Xin was named Xi Lu’s deputy; armed men entered the palace to arrest the empress.

sentences.zh: 閉戶藏壁中，歆就牽後出。

translations.literal: She closed the door and hid in a wall; Xin approached and dragged the empress out.
translations.idiomatic: She had barred her door and hidden inside a wall cavity; Hua Xin pulled her out.

sentences.zh: 時帝在外殿，引慮於坐。

translations.literal: At the time the emperor was in the outer hall, drawing Lu to sit.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor waited in the outer hall and had Xi Lu sit with him.

sentences.zh: 後被發徒跣行泣過訣曰：『不能復相活邪？

translations.literal: The empress, hair loose, barefoot, walking weeping passed in farewell, saying, ‘Can you not again keep each other alive?
translations.idiomatic: Disheveled and barefoot, she wept as she passed him, begging whether he could not save her life.

sentences.zh: 』帝曰：『我亦不知命在何時！

translations.literal: ’ The emperor said, ‘We too do not know when fate will be!
translations.idiomatic: He answered that he did not even know how long he himself might live.

sentences.zh: 』顧謂慮曰：『郗公，天下寧有是邪？

translations.literal: ’ He turned and said to Lu, ‘Lord Xi, can there be such in the empire?
translations.idiomatic: He turned to Xi Lu and asked whether such things could happen under Heaven.

sentences.zh: 』遂將後下暴室，以幽崩。

translations.literal: ’ Thereupon they led the empress down to the Blazing Room; by confinement she died.
translations.idiomatic: They took her to the palace prison and killed her in secret.

sentences.zh: 所生二皇子，皆鴆殺之。

translations.literal: The two imperial sons she bore were all poisoned to death.
translations.idiomatic: The two princes she had borne were poisoned as well.

sentences.zh: 後在位二十年，兄弟及宗族死者百餘人，母盈等十九人徙涿郡。

translations.literal: The empress having held position twenty years, younger brothers and clan kin who died were more than a hundred persons; mother Ying and nineteen others were moved to Zhuo commandery.
translations.idiomatic: She had been empress twenty years; over a hundred of her kin were executed, and nineteen people including her mother were banished to Zhuo commandery.

sentences.zh: 獻穆曹皇后

translations.literal: Empress Cao the Xianmu.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Cao of Emperor Xian, posthumously titled Xianmu

sentences.zh: 論曰：漢世皇后無謚，皆因帝謚以為稱。

translations.literal: The treatise says: In Han generations empresses had no posthumous titles; all followed the emperor’s posthumous title to make their designation.
translations.idiomatic: The historian remarks: Han empresses were not given posthumous titles of their own; they were known by the same posthumous phrase as their husband.

sentences.zh: 雖呂氏專政，上官臨制，亦無殊號。

translations.literal: Although the Lü clan monopolized government, the Shangguan faced and controlled, still there were no special designations.
translations.idiomatic: Even under the Lü regency and the Shangguan dictatorship no separate honorific was added.

sentences.zh: 中興，明帝始建光烈之稱，其後並以德為配，至於賢愚優劣，混同一貫，故馬、竇二後懼稱德焉。

translations.literal: At restoration, Emperor Ming first established the title Guanglie; afterward all took virtue as mate; as for worthy and foolish, excellent and inferior, mixed in one thread; therefore the two empresses Ma and Dou both feared being titled De.
translations.idiomatic: After the restoration Emperor Ming was the first to give his empress a distinct posthumous epithet, Guanglie; later rulers paired empresses with a virtue character regardless of merit, so both Empress Ma and Empress Dou were uneasy at receiving the word de in their titles.

sentences.zh: 其餘唯帝之庶母及蕃王承統，以追尊之重，特為其號，如恭懷、孝崇之比是也。

translations.literal: The rest: only the emperor’s secondary mothers and vassal kings who received the succession, by the weight of posthumous honor, had special titles made for them, such as Gonghuai, Xiaochong’s sort.
translations.idiomatic: Only imperial concubines raised posthumously or mothers of kings who inherited the throne received special titles such as Gonghuai or Xiaochong.

sentences.zh: 初平中，蔡邕始追正和熹之謚，其安思、順烈以下，皆依而加焉。

translations.literal: In Chuping, Cai Yong first pursued and corrected Hexi’s posthumous title; from Ansi, Shunlie downward, all followed and added.
translations.idiomatic: During Chuping Cai Yong corrected Empress Deng Sui’s posthumous title; empresses from Empress Yan (Ansi) and Empress Liang (Shunlie) onward were given similar additions.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：坤惟厚載，陰正乎內《詩》美好逑，《易》稱歸妹。

translations.literal: The encomium says: Kun is only thick-bearing; yin rectifies within. The Odes speaks of the fine mate; the Changes speaks of the returning younger sister.
translations.idiomatic: The verse encomium: Earth bears all in its breadth; the feminine principle rightfully rules within. The Book of Odes praises the good match; the Book of Changes names the Returning Maiden hexagram.

sentences.zh: 祁祁皇麗，言觀貞淑。

translations.literal: Stately imperial loveliness; in speech and bearing chaste and pure.
translations.idiomatic: Stately and radiant, their words and demeanor showed chaste virtue.

sentences.zh: 媚茲良哲，承我天祿。

translations.literal: Favor these good wise ones; receive our Heaven-granted fortune.
translations.idiomatic: Cherishing wise consorts, the throne received Heaven’s blessing.

sentences.zh: 班政蘭閨，宣禮椒屋。

translations.literal: They order government in the orchid boudoir; they proclaim rites in the pepper chamber.
translations.idiomatic: They ordered the inner chambers and taught ritual in the harem.

sentences.zh: 既雲德升，亦曰幸進。

translations.literal: Already it is said virtue ascends; also it is said favor advances.
translations.idiomatic: Some rose through merit; others through the emperor’s whim.

sentences.zh: 身當隆極，族漸河潤。

translations.literal: When the person reaches the utmost height, the clan gradually receives the river’s moisture.
translations.idiomatic: When a woman reached the highest rank, her whole clan drank from that stream of favor.

sentences.zh: 視景爭暉，方山並峻。

translations.literal: Gazing at sunlight contending in radiance; squared mountains together steep.
translations.idiomatic: They vied with the sun in splendor; their kin rose beside them like peaks in a range.

sentences.zh: 乘剛多阻，行地必順。

translations.literal: Riding hard yang has many obstacles; walking earth must be compliant.
translations.idiomatic: To press hard against strength invites peril; to move on earth one must yield and follow.

sentences.zh: 咎集驕滿，福協貞信。

translations.literal: Blame gathers in pride and fullness; fortune accords with steadfast faith.
translations.idiomatic: Disaster clings to arrogance; fortune favors constancy and trust.

sentences.zh: 慶延自己，禍成誰釁。

translations.literal: Blessing extends from oneself; disaster completed—whose fault?
translations.idiomatic: Good fortune springs from one’s own conduct; when ruin comes, who else is to blame?

sentences.zh: 附：公主

translations.literal: Appendix: princesses.
translations.idiomatic: Supplement: imperial princesses

sentences.zh: 漢制，皇女皆封縣公主，儀服同列侯。

translations.literal: By Han regulation, imperial daughters were all enfeoffed as county princesses; ritual dress matched full marquises.
translations.idiomatic: Under Han law every daughter of the emperor was titled princess of a county, with insignia equal to a full marquis.

sentences.zh: 其尊崇者，加號長公主，儀服同蕃王。

translations.literal: Those most honored had added title senior princess; ritual dress matched vassal kings.
translations.idiomatic: The most honored bore the additional title senior princess, with regalia like a vassal king’s.

sentences.zh: 諸王女皆封鄉、亭公主，儀服同鄉、亭侯。

translations.literal: The various kings’ daughters were all enfeoffed as village or pavilion princesses; ritual dress matched village or pavilion marquises.
translations.idiomatic: Daughters of kings were princesses of a village or pavilion, ranked like village or pavilion marquises.

sentences.zh: 肅宗唯特封東平憲王蒼、瑯邪孝王京女為縣公主。

translations.literal: Emperor Suzong alone specially enfeoffed the daughters of Prince Xian of Eastern Ping Cang and Prince Xiao of Langye Jing as county princesses.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Zhang alone made exceptions, enfeoffing the daughters of Liu Cang, the accomplished king of Eastern Ping, and Liu Jing, the filial king of Langye, as county princesses.

sentences.zh: 其後安帝、桓帝妹亦封長公主，同之皇女。

translations.literal: Afterward Emperor An’s and Emperor Huan’s younger sisters were also enfeoffed as senior princesses, the same as imperial daughters.
translations.idiomatic: Later the younger sisters of Emperors An and Huan were also titled senior princesses, on a par with the emperor’s own daughters.

sentences.zh: 其皇女封公主者，所生之子襲母封為列侯，皆傳國於後。

translations.literal: Those imperial daughters enfeoffed as princesses—their born sons inherited the mother’s enfeoffment as full marquises; all transmitted the state to posterity.
translations.idiomatic: When an imperial daughter held a princess title, her son could inherit her rank as a full marquis and pass the fief to his heirs.

sentences.zh: 鄉、亭之封，則不傳襲。

translations.literal: For village and pavilion enfeoffments, then they were not transmitted in succession.
translations.idiomatic: Village- and pavilion-level princess titles were not inherited.

sentences.zh: 其職僚品秩，事在《百官誌》。

translations.literal: Their offices, staff, and rank grades—the matter is in the Treatise on the Hundred Officials.
translations.idiomatic: Their offices and salary grades are set out in the Treatise on Officials.

sentences.zh: 不足別載，故附於後紀末。

translations.literal: Not sufficient to record separately; therefore appended at the end of the Later Annals.
translations.idiomatic: They do not warrant a separate chapter, so they are appended here at the end of the empresses’ annals.

sentences.zh: 世祖五女

translations.literal: Shizu’s five daughters.
translations.idiomatic: The five daughters of Emperor Guangwu (Shizu)

sentences.zh: 皇女義王，建武十五年封舞陽長公主，適陵鄉侯太仆梁松。

translations.literal: Imperial daughter Yiwang, in the fifteenth year of Jianwu enfeoffed as senior princess of Wuyang, married Grand Coachman Liang Song, Marquis of Lingxiang.
translations.idiomatic: The princess Yiwang was created senior princess of Wuyang in 39 and married Liang Song, grand coachman and marquis of Lingxiang.

sentences.zh: 松坐誹謗誅。

translations.literal: Song sitting guilt for slander was executed.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Song was executed for slander.

sentences.zh: 皇女中禮，十五年封涅陽公主，適顯親侯大鴻臚竇固，肅宗尊為長公主。

translations.literal: Imperial daughter Zhongli, in the fifteenth year enfeoffed princess of Neyang, married Dou Gu, Marquis of Xianqin, Grand Herald; Emperor Suzong honored her as senior princess.
translations.idiomatic: The princess Zhongli became princess of Neyang in 39 and married Grand Herald Dou Gu, marquis of Xianqin; Emperor Zhang later raised her to senior princess.

sentences.zh: 皇女紅夫，十五年封館陶公主，適駙馬都尉韓光。

translations.literal: Imperial daughter Hongfu, in the fifteenth year enfeoffed princess of Guantao, married Commandant of Attendant Cavalry Han Guang.
translations.idiomatic: The princess Hongfu was created princess of Guantao in 39 and married Han Guang, commandant of attendant cavalry.

sentences.zh: 光坐與淮陽王延謀反誅。

translations.literal: Guang sitting guilt for plotting rebellion with Prince Yan of Huaiyang was executed.
translations.idiomatic: Han Guang was executed for plotting rebellion with Liu Yan, prince of Huaiyang.

sentences.zh: 皇女禮劉，十七年封淯陽公主，適陽安侯長樂少府郭璜。

translations.literal: Imperial daughter Liliu, in the seventeenth year enfeoffed princess of Yuyang, married Guo Huang, Marquis of Yang’an, chamberlain for the palace of Chang-le.
translations.idiomatic: The princess Liliu was created princess of Yuyang in 41 and married Guo Huang, chamberlain for the Changle palace and marquis of Yang’an.

sentences.zh: 璜坐與竇憲謀反誅。

translations.literal: Huang sitting guilt for plotting rebellion with Dou Xian was executed.
translations.idiomatic: Guo Huang was executed for joining Dou Xian’s rebellion.

sentences.zh: 皇女綬，二十一年封酈邑公主，適新陽侯世子陰豐。

translations.literal: Imperial daughter Shou, in the twenty-first year enfeoffed princess of Liyi, married Yin Feng, heir of the Marquis of Xinyang.
translations.idiomatic: The princess Shou was created princess of Liyi in 45 and married Yin Feng, heir to the marquisate of Xinyang.

sentences.zh: 豐害主，誅死。

translations.literal: Feng harmed his mistress; he was executed to death.
translations.idiomatic: Yin Feng murdered her and was put to death.

sentences.zh: 世祖五女。

translations.literal: Shizu’s five daughters.
translations.idiomatic: Such were Emperor Guangwu’s five daughters.

sentences.zh: 顯宗十一女

translations.literal: Xianzong’s eleven daughters.
translations.idiomatic: The eleven daughters of Emperor Ming (Xianzong)

sentences.zh: 皇女姬，永平二年封獲嘉長公主，適楊邑侯將作大匠馮柱。

translations.literal: Imperial daughter Ji, in the second year of Yongping enfeoffed as senior princess of Huojia, married Feng Zhu, Marquis of Yangyi, master of works.
translations.idiomatic: The princess Ji was created senior princess of Huojia in 59 and married Feng Zhu, master of works and marquis of Yangyi.

sentences.zh: 皇女奴，三年封平陽公主，適大鴻臚馮順。

translations.literal: Imperial daughter Nu, in the third year enfeoffed princess of Pingyang, married Grand Herald Feng Shun.
translations.idiomatic: The princess Nu became princess of Pingyang in 60 and married Grand Herald Feng Shun.

sentences.zh: 皇女迎，三年封隆慮公主，適牟平侯耿襲。

translations.literal: Imperial daughter Ying, in the third year enfeoffed princess of Longlu, married Geng Xi, Marquis of Mouping.
translations.idiomatic: The princess Ying was created princess of Longlu in 60 and married Geng Xi, marquis of Mouping.

sentences.zh: 皇女次，三年封平氏公主。

translations.literal: Imperial daughter Ci, in the third year enfeoffed princess of Pingshi.
translations.idiomatic: The princess Ci was created princess of Pingshi in 60.

sentences.zh: 皇女致，三年封沁水公主。

translations.literal: Imperial daughter Zhi, in the third year enfeoffed princess of Qinshui.
translations.idiomatic: The princess Zhi was created princess of Qinshui in 60.

sentences.zh: 適高密侯鄧乾。

translations.literal: She married Deng Qian, Marquis of Gaomi.
translations.idiomatic: She married Deng Qian, marquis of Gaomi.

sentences.zh: 皇女小姬，十二年封平臯公主，適昌安侯侍中鄧蕃。

translations.literal: Imperial daughter Xiao Ji, in the twelfth year enfeoffed princess of Pinggao, married Deng Fan, Marquis of Chang’an, Palace Attendant.
translations.idiomatic: The princess Xiao Ji was created princess of Pinggao in 69 and married Deng Fan, palace attendant and marquis of Chang’an.

sentences.zh: 皇女仲，十七年封浚儀公主，適央侯黃門侍郎王度。

translations.literal: Imperial daughter Zhong, in the seventeenth year enfeoffed princess of Junyi, married Wang Du, Marquis of Yang, Gentleman-in-Attendance of the Yellow Gates.
translations.idiomatic: The princess Zhong became princess of Junyi in 74 and married Wang Du, gentleman at the Yellow Gates and marquis of Yang.

sentences.zh: 皇女惠，十七年封武安公主，適征羌侯世子黃門侍郎來棱，安帝尊為長公主。

translations.literal: Imperial daughter Hui, in the seventeenth year enfeoffed princess of Wu’an, married Lai Leng, heir of the Marquis of Zhengqiang, Gentleman-in-Attendance of the Yellow Gates; Emperor An honored her as senior princess.
translations.idiomatic: The princess Hui was created princess of Wu’an in 74 and married Lai Leng, heir to the Zhengqiang marquisate and gentleman at the Yellow Gates; Emperor An later elevated her to senior princess.

sentences.zh: 皇女小迎，元年封樂平公主。

translations.literal: Imperial daughter Xiao Ying, in the first year enfeoffed princess of Leping.
translations.idiomatic: The princess Xiao Ying was created princess of Leping in the first year of the reign (76 CE).

sentences.zh: 皇女小民，元年封成安公主。

translations.literal: Imperial daughter Xiao Min, in the first year enfeoffed princess of Cheng’an.
translations.idiomatic: The princess Xiao Min was created princess of Cheng’an in the first year of the reign (76 CE).

sentences.zh: 顯宗十一女。

translations.literal: Xianzong’s eleven daughters.
translations.idiomatic: Such were Emperor Ming’s eleven daughters.

sentences.zh: 肅宗三女

translations.literal: Suzong’s three daughters.
translations.idiomatic: The three daughters of Emperor Zhang (Suzong)

sentences.zh: 皇女男，建初四年封武德長公主。

translations.literal: Imperial daughter Nan, in the fourth year of Jianchu enfeoffed as senior princess of Wude.
translations.idiomatic: The princess Nan was created senior princess of Wude in 79.

sentences.zh: 皇女王，四年封平邑公主，適黃門侍郎馮由。

translations.literal: Imperial daughter Wang, in the fourth year enfeoffed princess of Pingyi, married Feng You, Gentleman-in-Attendance of the Yellow Gates.
translations.idiomatic: The princess Wang became princess of Pingyi in 79 and married Feng You, gentleman at the Yellow Gates.

sentences.zh: 皇女吉，永元五年封陰安公主。

translations.literal: Imperial daughter Ji, in the fifth year of Yongyuan enfeoffed princess of Yin’an.
translations.idiomatic: The princess Ji was created princess of Yin’an in 93.

sentences.zh: 肅宗三女。

translations.literal: Suzong’s three daughters.
translations.idiomatic: Such were Emperor Zhang’s three daughters.

sentences.zh: 和帝四女

translations.literal: Emperor He’s four daughters.
translations.idiomatic: The four daughters of Emperor He

sentences.zh: 皇女保，延平元年封脩武長公主。

translations.literal: Imperial daughter Bao, in the first year of Yanping enfeoffed as senior princess of Xiuwu.
translations.idiomatic: The princess Bao was created senior princess of Xiuwu in 106.

sentences.zh: 皇女成，元年封共邑公主。

translations.literal: Imperial daughter Cheng, in the first year enfeoffed princess of Gongyi.
translations.idiomatic: The princess Cheng was created princess of Gongyi in 106.

sentences.zh: 皇女利，元年封臨潁公主，適即墨侯侍中賈建。

translations.literal: Imperial daughter Li, in the first year enfeoffed princess of Linying, married Jia Jian, Marquis of Jimo, Palace Attendant.
translations.idiomatic: The princess Li became princess of Linying in 106 and married Jia Jian, palace attendant and marquis of Jimo.

sentences.zh: 皇女興，元年封聞喜公主。

translations.literal: Imperial daughter Xing, in the first year enfeoffed princess of Wenxi.
translations.idiomatic: The princess Xing was created princess of Wenxi in 106.

sentences.zh: 和帝四女。

translations.literal: Emperor He’s four daughters.
translations.idiomatic: Such were Emperor He’s four daughters.

sentences.zh: 順帝三女

translations.literal: Emperor Shun’s three daughters.
translations.idiomatic: The three daughters of Emperor Shun

sentences.zh: 皇女生，永和三年封舞陽長公主。

translations.literal: Imperial daughter Sheng, in the third year of Yonghe enfeoffed as senior princess of Wuyang.
translations.idiomatic: The princess Sheng was created senior princess of Wuyang in 128.

sentences.zh: 皇女成男，三年封冠軍長公主。

translations.literal: Imperial daughter Chengnan, in the third year enfeoffed as senior princess of Guanjun.
translations.idiomatic: The princess Chengnan was created senior princess of Guanjun in 128.

sentences.zh: 皇女廣，永和六年封汝陽長公主。

translations.literal: Imperial daughter Guang, in the sixth year of Yonghe enfeoffed as senior princess of Ruyang.
translations.idiomatic: The princess Guang was created senior princess of Ruyang in 131.

sentences.zh: 順帝三女。

translations.literal: Emperor Shun’s three daughters.
translations.idiomatic: Such were Emperor Shun’s three daughters.

sentences.zh: 桓帝三女

translations.literal: Emperor Huan’s three daughters.
translations.idiomatic: The three daughters of Emperor Huan

sentences.zh: 皇女華，延熹元年封陽安長公主，適不其侯輔國將軍伏完。

translations.literal: Imperial daughter Hua, in the first year of Yanxi enfeoffed as senior princess of Yang’an, married Fu Wan, Marquis of Buqi, Supporting-State General.
translations.idiomatic: The princess Hua was created senior princess of Yang’an in 158 and married Fu Wan, supporting-state general and marquis of Buqi.

sentences.zh: 皇女堅，七年封潁陰長公主。

translations.literal: Imperial daughter Jian, in the seventh year enfeoffed as senior princess of Yingyin.
translations.idiomatic: The princess Jian was created senior princess of Yingyin in 164.

sentences.zh: 皇女脩，七年封陽翟長公主。

translations.literal: Imperial daughter Xiu, in the seventh year enfeoffed as senior princess of Yangzhai.
translations.idiomatic: The princess Xiu was created senior princess of Yangzhai in 164.

sentences.zh: 桓帝三女。

translations.literal: Emperor Huan’s three daughters.
translations.idiomatic: Such were Emperor Huan’s three daughters.

sentences.zh: 靈帝一女

translations.literal: Emperor Ling’s one daughter.
translations.idiomatic: The one daughter of Emperor Ling

sentences.zh: 皇女某，光和三年封萬年公主。

translations.literal: Imperial daughter so-and-so, in the third year of Guanghe enfeoffed princess of Wannian.
translations.idiomatic: A princess whose name was not recorded was created princess of Wannian in 180.

sentences.zh: 靈帝一女。

translations.literal: Emperor Ling’s one daughter.
translations.idiomatic: Such was Emperor Ling’s only daughter.
